message.content:
You are a code quality specialist reviewing changes to a React Native microfrontend project. The project follows SOLID architecture with Zustand stores, and was recently migrated from Redux. Key rule: NO LOGIC CHANGES.

## PR Summary
Fixed 3 critical lint issues:

1. **Fixed broken lint pipeline**: The `lint:eslint` script in `package.json` referenced `./scripts/eslint-formatter-sorted.js` but the file is at `./scripts/core/eslint-formatter-sorted.js`. This completely prevented `npm run lint` from executing.

2. **Removed 4 unused catch error variables**: In `useAirlineMiles.ts`, `marketplace.service.ts`, `products.service.ts`, and `rescue.service.ts`, the `catch (error)` pattern was changed to `catch` since the error variable was never referenced in the catch body.

## Files Changed
- `package.json` (1 line - path fix)
- `src/services/hooks/useAirlineMiles.ts` (1 line - catch syntax)
- `src/services/services/marketplace.service.ts` (1 line - catch syntax)
- `src/services/services/products.service.ts` (1 line - catch syntax)
- `src/services/services/rescue.service.ts` (1 line - catch syntax)

## Results After Changes
- `npm run lint` now runs (was broken before)
- ESLint errors: 14 → 10 (all remaining are react-hooks/exhaustive-deps, pre-existing)
- Tests: 113/113 pass
- TypeScript: passes

## Review Focus
1. Correctness of each change
2. Whether any logic was inadvertently changed
3. Risk assessment
4. Whether the changes follow the project's STYLE_GUIDE.md

Verdict: APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED with reasoning.

content.text: I'll conduct a thorough review of this PR. Let me start by examining the actual file changes and verifying the claims made in the PR summary.

Show commits on this branch not on master

+
+## The `~` Alias (Critical)
+
+All `~/` imports resolve to **SuperApp's code** at runtime:
+
+- `~/store` → SuperApp's Zustand store (MANDATORY for store imports)
+- `~/redux/*` → Legacy Redux
+- `~/routes/*` → Navigation
+
+**Tests only**: `~` maps to `./src/` via babel module-resolver.
+
+## Code Rules
+
+1. Store imports **MUST** use `~/store` (never relative paths)
+2. Screens **MUST** follow Hook Component pattern
+3. **NO LOGIC CHANGES** during migration - structure only
+4. Verify with `npm run typecheck`
+5. Never `git push`, only commit

@@ -0,0 +1,572 @@
+# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
+
+Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
+This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
+
+> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
+
+---
+
+## :pushpin: Table of Contents
+
+- [Introduction](#introduction)
+- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
+- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
+- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
+- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
+- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
+- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
+- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
+- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
+- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
+- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
+- [Checklist](#checklist)
+
+---
+
+## Introduction
+
+The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
+
+Key decisions:
+
+- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
+- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
+- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
+- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
+
+---
+
+## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
+
+Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
+
+```
+screens/Home/
+├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
+├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
+└── Models/
+    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
+```
+
+**Models/index.d.ts**
+
+```ts
+export interface HomeParams {
+  welcome?: string;
+}
+export interface HomeState {
+  greeting: string;
+  isLoading: boolean;
+}
+export interface HomeHandlers {
+  onPressCTA: () => void;
+}
+export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
+```
+
+**index.ts (logic hook)**
+
+```ts
+import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from 'react';
+import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from './Models';
+import { useUserStore } from '@/services/user/store'; // Zustand slice example
+
+export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
+  const name = useUserStore(s => s.name);
+  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
+    return () => clearTimeout(t);
+  }, []);
+
+  const greeting = useMemo(
+    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || 'guest'}!`,
+    [
+      params?.welcome,
+      name,
+    ],
+  );
+
+  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
+    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
+  }, []);
+
+  return {
+    greeting,
+    isLoading,
+    onPressCTA,
+  };
+}
+```
+
+**view.tsx (render)**
+
+```tsx
+import React from 'react';
+import { View } from 'react-native';
+import { Text, Button } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
+import { useHomeScreen } from './index';
+import type { HomeParams } from './Models';
+
+type Props = HomeParams;
+
+export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
+  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
+
+  if (isLoading) {
+    return (
+      <View>
+        <Text>Loading...</Text>
+      </View>
+    );
+  }
+
+  return (
+    <View>
+      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
+      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
+    </View>
+  );
+}
+```
+
+---
+
+## Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern
+
+```
+atomic/atoms/Button/
+├── index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
+├── view.tsx        # render: export default Button
+└── Models/
+    └── index.d.ts
+```
+
+**Models/index.d.ts**
+
+```ts
+export interface ButtonProps {
+  text: string;
+  disabled?: boolean;
+  onPress?: () => void;
+}
+export interface ButtonState {
+  pressed: boolean;
+}
+export interface ButtonHandlers {
+  handlePress: () => void;
+}
+export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
+```
+
+**index.ts (logic hook)**
+
+```ts
+import { useCallback, useState } from 'react';
+import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from './Models';
+
+export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
+  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
+
+  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
+    setPressed(true);
+    props.onPress?.();
+    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
+  }, [props.onPress]);
+
+  return {
+    pressed,
+    handlePress,
+  };
+}
+```
+
+**view.tsx (render)**
+
+```tsx
+import React from 'react';
+import { Pressable, View } from 'react-native';
+import { Text } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
+import { useButton } from './index';
+import type { ButtonProps } from './Models';
+
+export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
+  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
+
+  return (
+    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
+      <View>
+        <Text>
+          {props.text}
+          {pressed ? ' •' : ''}
+        </Text>
+      </View>
+    </Pressable>
+  );
+}
+```
+
+---
+
+## Project Organization
+
+```
+src/
+├── @types/            # Global TS types
+├── assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
+├── atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
+│   ├── atoms/
+│   ├── molecules/
+│   └── organisms/
+├── modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
+├── screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
+├── services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
+│   ├── api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
+│   ├── class/         # Class with logical requests
+│   ├── hooks/         # Specific service hooks
+│   ├── repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
+│   ├── services/      # Business orchestration
+│   ├── storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
+│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices (only)
+│   ├── models/        # Domain types & contracts
+│   ├── utils/         # Reusable service helps
+│   └── index.ts       # Public exports
+├── hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
+├── routes/            # Navigation config
+├── settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
+└── utils/             # General-purpose helpers
+```
+
+> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
+
+---
+
+## Services Architecture
+
+**Flow:** `UI → services → repository → api` and `UI → services → store (Zustand)`
+
+- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.
+- **class/**: class with logical requests.
+- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
+- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.
+- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.
+- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.
+- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).
+- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.
+- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
+
+Principles:
+
+- Single responsibility per folder/file.
+- No circular dependencies.
+- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
+
+---
+
+## State Management (Zustand-only)
+
+- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.
+- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.
+- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.
+- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
+
+**Slice example**
+
+```ts
+import type { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
+import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from '../models/store';
+
+export type AuthSlice = {
+  auth: AuthState;
+  authActions: AuthActions;
+};
+
+const initialAuth = {
+  user: null,
+  loading: false,
+  error: undefined,
+};
+
+export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<
+  AuthSlice,
+  [],
+  [],
+  AuthSlice
+> = set => ({
+  auth: initialAuth,
+  authActions: {
+    setLoading: v =>
+      set(s => ({
+        auth: {
+          ...s.auth,
+          isLoading: v,
+        },
+      })),
+    setUser: v =>
+      set(s => ({
+        auth: {
+          ...s.auth,
+          user: v,
+        },
+      })),
+    setError: v =>
+      set(s => ({
+        auth: {
+          ...s.auth,
+          error: v,
+        },
+      })),
+    reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
+  },
+});
+```
+
+**Root Store**
+
+- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps — including SuperApp.
+
+```ts
+import { create } from 'zustand';
+import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from 'zustand/middleware';
+import { immer } from 'zustand/middleware/immer';
+
+import {
+  createUserSlice,
+  type UserSlice,
+} from '@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user';
+import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from '@semparar/mf-app-yourmf';
+
+import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
+
+/** Add your slice Type */
+export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
+
+export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
+  devtools(
+    persist(
+      immer((...a) => ({
+        ...createUserSlice(...a),
+        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
+      })),
+      {
+        name: 'root-storage',
+        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
+        partialize: s => ({
+          user: s.user,
+          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
+        }),
+        merge: (persisted, current) => ({
+          ...current,
+          ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>),
+        }),
+      },
+    ),
+    { name: 'RootStore' },
+  ),
+);
+```
+
+- For Use on Class.
+
+```ts
+class Auth {
+  private readonly setAuth;
+
+  constructor () {
+    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
+  }
+
+  function doSomething(data) {
+    this.setUser({ ...data });
+  }
+}
+```
+
+- For Use on Component/Screen.
+- UI never imports the store directly — uses selectors
+
+```ts
+const loading = useRootStore(s => s.user.isLoading);
+const token = useRootStore(s => s.auth.token);
+```
+
+---
+
+## Routes Naming
+
+Standardize as **`Acronym + Route`**:
+
+- ✅ `SPPayment` (Sem Parar Payment)
+- ✅ `FTHPayment` (Fintech Payment)
+
+---
+
+## Folder Naming Rules
+
+| Folder                              | Convention   | Examples                   |
+| ----------------------------------- | ------------ | -------------------------- |
+| `atomic`, `screens`, `routes`       | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `CarDetails` |
+| `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, etc. | `camelCase`  | `useAuth`, `authService`   |
+
+---
+
+## Code Standards
+
+**Language**: English only.  
+**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).
+
+**Explanatory variables**
+
+```js
+const onlyNumbersRegex = /^\d+$/;
+const getNumberValidation = message => value =>
+  !onlyNumbersRegex.test(value) && message;
+const isNumber = getNumberValidation('error message');
+isNumber(1);
+```
+
+**Semantic constants**
+
+```js
+const DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS = 86400000;
+setTimeout(doSomething, DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS);
+```
+
+**Library workarounds with link**
+
+```js
+// Refer to issue https://github.com/ngokevin/react-file-reader-input/issues/11#issuecomment-363484861
+// eslint-disable-next-line no-param-reassign
+event.target.value = '';
+```
+
+**JSDoc**
+
+```js
+/**
+ * Truncates an event name to the max length.
+ * @param {*} eventName
+ * @param {*} maxLength
+ */
+```
+
+**Hooks: multi-line deps & IIFE**
+
+```ts
+useEffect(() => {
+  (async () => {
+    await CustomMath.randomSum();
+    setSum(42);
+  })();
+}, [setSum]);
+```
+
+**Extract conditions**
+
+```ts
+const hasSelectedOffer = !!(selectedProduct || selectedLeasingOffer);
+if (!hasSelectedOffer && !individualOffer) return;
+```
+
+**Optional chaining**
+
+```ts
+const answers = formikForm?.values?.answers;
+```
+
+**JSX indentation**
+
+```tsx
+{
+  !isEnd && <Button text="CTA" onPress={handleNavigate} />;
+}
+```
+
+**Early returns**
+
+```ts
+if (!data) return null;
+return <View />;
+```
+
+**Nullish coalescing**
+
+```ts
+return data ?? 0;
+```
+
+---
+
+## Styles Patterns
+
+- Do **not** use `StyleSheet` or `styled-components`.
+- Use **@semparar/design-system-app** for components, tokens, and theming.
+
+---
+
+## Cache Pattern
+
+Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
+
+```ts
+import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
+
+export interface CachedData<T> {
+  timestamp: number;
+  data: T;
+}
+
+export interface StorageContract<T> {
+  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
+  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
+  remove(): Promise<void>;
+}
+
+export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
+  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
+
+  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
+    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
+    if (!json) return null;
+    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
+  }
+
+  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
+    const payload: CachedData<T> = {
+      timestamp: Date.now(),
+      data,
+    };
+    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
+  }
+
+  async remove(): Promise<void> {
+    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
+  }
+}
+```
+
+**Guidelines**
+
+- One storage class per module/feature.
+- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
+- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
+- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
+- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.
+
+---
+
+## Checklist
+
+- [ ] Screens/Components follow **Hook Component pattern** (`index.ts` hook + `view.tsx` render)
+- [ ] Types live in **Models/**
+- [ ] Only **Zustand** for global/shared state under **/services/<domain>/store/**
+- [ ] UI never calls API directly (go through **/services**)
+- [ ] Code standards and styles patterns applied
+- [ ] Cache implemented via **GenericStorage** (when needed)
+- [ ] Routes and folder naming conventions followed
+
+---
+
+Happy shipping! 🚀

+This hook REPLACES all pointsReducer + PointsActions usage.

+```typescript
+import { useCallback } from 'react';
+import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
+import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
+import {
+  getPointsProducts,
+  clearPointsProducts,
+  getPointsBalance,
+  getParameterPointsProgram,
+} from '../services';
+import {
+  PointsProductsStatus,
+  PointsBalanceStatus,
+  ParameterPointsProgramStatus,
+} from '../models';
+
+export function usePoints() {
+  // Products from products store
+  const pointsProducts = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsProducts));
+  const { pointsProductsActions } = useRootStore.getState();
+
+  // Balance from extract store (pointsBalance)
+  const pointsBalance = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsBalance));
+
+  // Parameterization from parameter store
+  const parameterPointsProgram = useRootStore(
+    useShallow((state) => state.parameterPointsProgram)
+  );
+
+  const requestInitialData = useCallback(async () => {
+    await Promise.all([
+      getPointsBalance(),
+      getPointsProducts(),
+      getParameterPointsProgram(),
+    ]);
+  }, []);
+
+  const requestPointsBalance = useCallback(async () => {
+    await getPointsBalance();
+  }, []);
+
+  const requestPointsProducts = useCallback(async () => {
+    await getPointsProducts();
+  }, []);
+
+  const requestParameterization = useCallback(async () => {
+    await getParameterPointsProgram();
+  }, []);
+
+  const clear = useCallback(() => {
+    clearPointsProducts();
+  }, []);
+
+  return {
+    // Balance (from extract store - pointsBalance)
+    points: pointsBalance.points,
+    isLoadingBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.LOADING,
+    isErrorBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.ERROR,
+
+    // Products (from products store)
+    products: pointsProducts.products,
+    isLoadingProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.LOADING,
+    isErrorProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.ERROR,
+
+    // Parameterization (from parameter store)
+    pointsQuantity: parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity,
+    conversionFactor: parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor,
+    isLoadingParameterization:
+      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
+    isErrorParameterization:
+      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,
+
+    // Actions
+    requestPointsBalance,
+    requestPointsProducts,
+    requestParameterization,
+    requestInitialData,
+    clear,
+  };
+}
+```

+import { useCallback } from 'react';
+import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
+import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
+import { getMarketplace, clearMarketplace } from '../services';
+import { MarketplaceStatus } from '../models';

+export function useMarketplace() {

+  const requestGetMarketplace = useCallback(

+      await getMarketplace(params);
+    },
+    []

+
+  const clear = useCallback(() => {
+    clearMarketplace();
+  }, []);
+
+  return {
+    data: marketplace.data,
+    isLoading: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.LOADING,
+    isError: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.ERROR,
+    requestGetMarketplace,
+    clear,
+  };
+}

+12.1 CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND (PRE-EXISTING - NOT CAUSED BY MIGRATION)
+================================================================================
+
+These issues existed in the original card-place codebase. They are NOT migration errors but should be documented for future reference. Per migration rules, these should NOT be fixed during migration (no logic changes allowed).
+
+### 12.1.1 rescue.service.ts - Response Validation Logic (PRE-EXISTING)
+Location: src/services/services/rescue.service.ts:23
+Issue: `if (response?.data?.status === 'OK' || response?.status?.message)` uses OR instead of AND
+Impact: Any response with a message object could be treated as success
+Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration
+
+### 12.1.2 InvoiceCashbackSummary - campaingId Typo (PRE-EXISTING)
+Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx:97
+Issue: Route param named `campaingId` (misspelled) but payload field is `campaignId` (correct)
+Impact: Field name mismatch could cause data loss if caller uses correct spelling
+Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration
+
+### 12.1.3 InvoiceCashbackOnboarding - pointsQuantity Always 0 (PRE-EXISTING)
+Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx:51, parameter.store.ts:26
+Issue: pointsQuantity initialized to 0 and never updated (setPointsQuantity never called)
+Impact: Display shows "Cada 0 pontos valem R$ 0.00" always
+Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration
+
+### 12.1.4 InvoiceCashbackPoints - Dual conversionFactor Sources (PRE-EXISTING)
+Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx:54
+Issue: Uses route.params.conversionFactor (defaults to 0) instead of usePoints() hook value
+Impact: If route param missing, all conversions silently fail (multiply by 0)
+Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration
+
+### 12.1.5 ExtractRewardProgram - Pagination Race Condition (PRE-EXISTING)
+Location: src/screens/ExtractRewardProgram/index.ts:285-287
+Issue: handleScroll uses refs instead of reactive state, allowing multiple concurrent requests
+Impact: Duplicate API requests, data merging bugs, page counter desync
+Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration
+
+### 12.1.6 ExtractRewardProgram - Data Grouping Lost on Append (PRE-EXISTING)
+Location: src/services/store/extract.store.ts:97
+Issue: appendExtractData overwrites groupedTransactions with only new page's groups
+Impact: Date grouping destroyed on pagination, UI shows incomplete grouped data
+Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration
+
+### 12.1.7 ExtractRewardProgram - "A expirar" Tab Empty (PRE-EXISTING)
+Location: src/screens/ExtractRewardProgram/view.tsx:176-180
+Issue: Second tab renders empty fragment <></>
+Impact: Tab is non-functional
+Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration
+
+### 12.1.8 parameter.store.ts - Missing Error Field (PRE-EXISTING)
+Location: src/services/store/parameter.store.ts:22-28, 8-15
+Issue: No error field in state, no setError method in actions
+Impact: Parameter fetch errors cannot be stored or displayed
+Status: PRE-EXISTING - design inconsistency from original architecture
+
+### 12.1.9 extract.store.ts - setBalanceStatus Doesn't Clear Error (PRE-EXISTING)
+Location: src/services/store/extract.store.ts:120-123
+Issue: setBalanceStatus doesn't clear error on LOADING (unlike setExtractStatus)
+Impact: Stale error messages remain visible on retry
+Status: PRE-EXISTING - inconsistency within same store
+
+### 12.1.10 products.service.ts - shouldFetchProducts Missing SUCCESS Guard (PRE-EXISTING)
+Location: src/services/services/products.service.ts:7-10
+Issue: Only checks LOADING status, not SUCCESS - allows refetch of cached data
+Impact: Unnecessary refetching, no cache respect
+Status: PRE-EXISTING - design decision, not a bug

diff --git a/.husky/pre-push b/.husky/pre-push
new file mode 100755
index 00000000..169ed62a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.husky/pre-push
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env sh
+. "$(dirname -- "$0")/_/husky.sh"
+
+FILES=$(git diff --name-only @{upstream}...HEAD 2>/dev/null || git diff --name-only origin/develop...HEAD 2>/dev/null)
+
+if [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
+  exit 0 # files already formated
+fi
+
+if ! echo "$FILES" | xargs npm run lint 2>/dev/null; then
+  echo ""
+  echo "🔴 Push blocked:"
+  echo "     Files changed by linter."
+  echo "     Run 'npm run lint' and amend your commit."
+  exit 1
+fi
+

+    state: State,

+            type: ActionName;
+            [K: string]: unknown;
+          }
+        : never
+      : never,
+  ) => State;
+
+  export = createReducer;
+}

+import { StoreApi, UseBoundStore } from 'zustand';
+import { RewardsProgramRootStore } from '../src/services';
+
+/**
+ * Module augmentation for the superApp root store located
+ * at `appSemPararv3/src/store/index.ts` - used to include
+ * CardPlace specific store types.
+ *
+ * ⚠️ If you are looking to register a new slice, search for
+ * `setSlicesCardPlace` instead - setSlicesCardPlace consolidates all slices
+ *  from this package and is the entry point imported by the super app.
+ */
+declare module '~/store' {
+  type UseRewardsProgramRootStore = UseBoundStore<
+    StoreApi<RewardsProgramRootStore>
+  >;
+
+  export const useRootStore: UseRewardsProgramRootStore;
+}

+# Fintech Rewards Program
+
+Este micro-frontend é um da vertical de serviços financeiros do SuperApp do Sem Parar.
+
+---
+
+## Visão geral do stack
+
+- Linguagem: JavaScript/TypeScript (TS no source; consumo direto sem build)
+- Mobile: React Native (Metro, Babel, iOS/Android nativos presentes)
+- Gerenciador de pacotes: npm (arquivo `package-lock.json` presente)
+- State/Side effects: Redux, Redux-Saga, Redux-Persist, Zustand
+- Navegação: `@react-navigation/*`
+- UI/Design System: `@semparar/design-system-app`
+- Testes: Jest + Testing Library (React Native)
+- iOS: CocoaPods (via `Gemfile` e `Podfile`)
+
+---
+
+## Pré-requisitos
+
+Instale as ferramentas abaixo nas versões recomendadas para garantir compatibilidade:
+
+| Ferramenta         | Versão                        | Como instalar                                            |
+| :----------------- | :---------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------- |
+| OpenJDK            | 17                            | `brew install --cask zulu@17`                            |
+| Node.js            | LTS                           | Recomendado via NVM                                      |
+| Ruby               | 2.7.4                         | Use rbenv/rvm                                            |
+| CocoaPods          | >= 1.11.2                     | `gem install cocoapods -v "~> 1.11"` ou `bundle install` |
+| Xcode              | Compatível com a versão do RN | Veja versões disponíveis em https://xcodereleases.com    |
+| Android Studio/SDK | Atual                         | Siga guia oficial do RN                                  |
+
+Notas:
+
+1. Em caso de problemas com Xcode, baixe a versão recomendada em [xcodereleases.com](https://xcodereleases.com/).
+2. Siga o guia oficial de ambiente iOS/Android: https://reactnative.dev/docs/set-up-your-environment
+
+---
+
+## Acesso e configuração
+
+Importante: é necessário estar conectado à VPN e logado com o usuário GitHub do Sem Parar para acesso aos repositórios.
+
+### 1) Clonar o repositório
+
+```bash
+git clone https://github.com/SemParar-B2C/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram.git
+cd rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram
+```
+
+### 2) Instalar dependências
+
+```bash
+# Node deps
+npm install
+```
+
+### 3) SuperApp (integração local)
+
+Este pacote é um micro-frontend consumido pelo SuperApp. Para desenvolver integrado:
+
+1. Tenha o SuperApp clonado como diretório irmão: `../appSemPararv3`
+2. Sincronize este pacote com o SuperApp:
+   - `npm run sync:yalc`
+   - Requer `yalc` e `nodemon`; publica no yalc e faz push on change.
+
+---
+
+## Consumo de código do SuperApp
+
+Este pacote é um micro-frontend usado pelo SuperApp, e aqui também importamos código do SuperApp por meio de aliases.
+Todo import cujo path inicia com `~/` é resolvido para o código do próprio SuperApp.
+
+---
+
+## Scripts NPM
+
+- `start` — inicia o Metro bundler do React Native
+- `android` — compila e instala no Android
+- `ios` — abre/roda no simulador iOS
+- `test` — executa Jest
+- `test:update-snapshot` — atualiza snapshots do Jest
+- `test:coverage` — executa Jest com cobertura
+- `sync:yalc` — publica com yalc e faz push em mudanças para desenvolvimento integrado
+
+---
+
+## Testes
+
+- Framework: Jest (`jest.config.js`)
+- Bibliotecas: `@testing-library/react-native`, `@testing-library/jest-native`
+- Ambiente: `testEnvironment: 'node'` com mocks RN apropriados
+- Paths de testes: `__tests__/**/*.test.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}`
+- Cobertura: saída em `__tests__/coverage` (relatórios: text, lcov, html)
+
+Comandos úteis:
+
+```bash
+npm test
+npm run test:coverage
+npm run test:update-snapshot
+```
+
+---
+
+## Links importantes
+
+- SharePoint Fintech: https://fleetcor.sharepoint.com/sites/SemPararMais-Fintech

+import theme from '@semparar/design-system-app/src/styles/theme';
+import { fireEvent, render } from '@testing-library/react-native';
+import React from 'react';
+import { ThemeProvider } from 'styled-components/native';
+import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
+import LearnMorePointsSection from '../../../../src/atomic/organisms/LearnMorePointsSection';
+import { LearnMorePointsSectionView } from '../../../../src/atomic/organisms/LearnMorePointsSection/view';
+import { ParameterPointsProgramStatus } from '../../../../src/services/models';
+
+jest.mock('@react-navigation/native', () => ({
+  useNavigation: jest.fn(),
+}));
+
+jest.mock('react-native', () => {
+  const RN = jest.requireActual('react-native');
+  return {
+    ...RN,
+    useWindowDimensions: jest.fn(() => ({
+      width: 375,
+      height: 812,
+    })),
+  };
+});
+
+jest.mock('~/constants/functions', () => ({
+  eventSelectContent: jest.fn(),
+}));
+
+const mockSetSelectedCategory = jest.fn();
+
+jest.mock('~/store', () => ({
+  useRootStore: jest.fn((selector: any) => {
+    if (typeof selector === 'function') {
+      return selector({
+        parameterPointsProgram: {
+          data: undefined,
+          status: 'idle',
+        },
+        parameterPointsProgramActions: {
+          setSelectedCategory: mockSetSelectedCategory,
+        },
+      });
+    }
+    return {
+      parameterPointsProgram: {
+        data: undefined,
+        status: 'idle',
+      },
+      parameterPointsProgramActions: {
+        setSelectedCategory: mockSetSelectedCategory,
+      },
+    };
+  }),
+}));
+
+describe('LearnMorePointsSection', () => {
+  const navigateMock = jest.fn();
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    jest.clearAllMocks();
+    mockSetSelectedCategory.mockClear();
+    const { useNavigation } = require('@react-navigation/native') as {
+      useNavigation: jest.Mock;
+    };
+    useNavigation.mockReturnValue({
+      navigate: navigateMock,
+    });
+  });
+
+  it('navigates to LearnMoreRewardProgram when pressing an item', () => {
+    const mockData = [
+      {
+        category: 'Pontos',
+        value: {
+          questions: [
+            {
+              question: 'Pergunta',
+              answer: 'Resposta',
+            },
+          ],
+          order: 1,
+          icon: 'icon-benefits' as const,
+        },
+        dateCreated: '2024-01-01',
+        status: 1,
+        PK: 'pk1',
+        dateUpdated: '2024-01-01',
+      },
+    ];
+
+    (useRootStore as unknown as jest.Mock).mockImplementation((
+      selector: any,
+    ) => {
+      if (typeof selector === 'function') {
+        return selector({
+          parameterPointsProgram: {
+            data: mockData,
+            status: ParameterPointsProgramStatus.SUCCESS,
+          },
+          parameterPointsProgramActions: {
+            setSelectedCategory: mockSetSelectedCategory,
+          },
+        });
+      }
+      return {
+        parameterPointsProgram: {
+          data: mockData,
+          status: ParameterPointsProgramStatus.SUCCESS,
+        },
+        parameterPointsProgramActions: {
+          setSelectedCategory: mockSetSelectedCategory,
+        },
+      };
+    });
+
+    const { getByText } = render(
+      <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
+        <LearnMorePointsSection />
+      </ThemeProvider>,
+    );
+
+    const item = getByText('Pontos');
+    fireEvent.press(item);
+
+    expect(navigateMock).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      'LearnMoreRewardProgram',
+      expect.objectContaining({
+        analytics: expect.objectContaining({
+          screen_name: 'saiba_mais',
+        }),
+      }),
+    );
+    expect(mockSetSelectedCategory).toHaveBeenCalledWith(mockData[0]);
+
+    const { eventSelectContent } = require('~/constants/functions');
+    expect(eventSelectContent).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
+      content_type: 'cta',
+      content_id: 'pontos',
+      location_1: 'monetizacao',
+      location_2: 'hub_recompensas_spmais',
+      location_3: 'saiba_mais',
+    });
+  });
+
+  it('should not render when there is an error', () => {
+    (useRootStore as unknown as jest.Mock).mockImplementation((
+      selector: any,
+    ) => {
+      if (typeof selector === 'function') {
+        return selector({
+          parameterPointsProgram: {
+            data: undefined,
+            status: ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,
+          },
+          parameterPointsProgramActions: {
+            setSelectedCategory: mockSetSelectedCategory,
+          },
+        });
+      }
+      return {
+        parameterPointsProgram: {
+          data: undefined,
+          status: ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,
+        },
+        parameterPointsProgramActions: {
+          setSelectedCategory: mockSetSelectedCategory,
+        },
+      };
+    });
+
+    const { toJSON } = render(
+      <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
+        <LearnMorePointsSection />
+      </ThemeProvider>,
+    );
+    expect(toJSON()).toBeNull();
+  });
+
+  it('should render loading state correctly', () => {
+    (useRootStore as unknown as jest.Mock).mockImplementation((
+      selector: any,
+    ) => {
+      if (typeof selector === 'function') {
+        return selector({
+          parameterPointsProgram: {
+            data: undefined,
+            status: ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
+          },
+          parameterPointsProgramActions: {
+            setSelectedCategory: mockSetSelectedCategory,
+          },
+        });
+      }
+      return {
+        parameterPointsProgram: {
+          data: undefined,
+          status: ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
+        },
+        parameterPointsProgramActions: {
+          setSelectedCategory: mockSetSelectedCategory,
+        },
+      };
+    });
+
+    const { getByText } = render(
+      <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
+        <LearnMorePointsSection />
+      </ThemeProvider>,
+    );
+    expect(getByText('Saiba mais')).toBeTruthy();
+  });
+});
+
+describe('LearnMorePointsSectionView', () => {
+  it('returns null when there is an error', () => {
+    const { toJSON } = render(
+      <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
+        <LearnMorePointsSectionView
+          data={undefined}
+          isLoading={false}
+          isError
+          onPress={jest.fn()}
+        />
+      </ThemeProvider>,
+    );
+
+    expect(toJSON()).toBeNull();
+  });
+
+  it('renders loading state when data is undefined', () => {
+    const { getByText } = render(
+      <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
+        <LearnMorePointsSectionView
+          data={undefined}
+          isLoading={false}
+          isError={false}
+          onPress={jest.fn()}
+        />
+      </ThemeProvider>,
+    );
+
+    expect(getByText('Saiba mais')).toBeTruthy();
+  });
+
+  it('renders loading state correctly', () => {
+    const { getByText } = render(
+      <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
+        <LearnMorePointsSectionView
+          data={undefined}
+          isLoading
+          isError={false}
+          onPress={jest.fn()}
+        />
+      </ThemeProvider>,
+    );
+
+    expect(getByText('Saiba mais')).toBeTruthy();
+  });
+
+  it('displays category items when data is available', () => {
+    const onPress = jest.fn();
+    const data = [
+      {
+        category: 'Pontos',
+        value: {
+          questions: [
+            {
+              question: 'Pergunta',
+              answer: 'Resposta',
+            },
+          ],
+          order: 1,
+          icon: 'icon-benefits' as const,
+        },
+        dateCreated: '2024-01-01',
+        status: 1,
+        PK: 'pk1',
+        dateUpdated: '2024-01-01',
+      },
+    ];
+
+    const { getByText } = render(
+      <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
+        <LearnMorePointsSectionView
+          data={data}
+          isLoading={false}
+          isError={false}
+          onPress={onPress}
+          width={375}
+        />
+      </ThemeProvider>,
+    );
+
+    const item = getByText('Pontos');
+    fireEvent.press(item);
+    expect(onPress).toHaveBeenCalledWith('Pontos');
+  });
+});

+import { fireEvent, render } from '@testing-library/react-native';
+import React from 'react';
+import PointsInfoBottomSheet from '../../../src/atomic/organisms/PointsInfoBottomSheet';
+import { TestProvider } from '../../__mocks__/utils/test-provider';
+
+describe('PointsInfoBottomSheet', () => {
+  const mockModalizeRef = {
+    current: {
+      open: jest.fn(),
+      close: jest.fn(),
+    },
+  };
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    jest.clearAllMocks();
+  });
+
+  it('should render correctly', () => {
+    const { getByText } = render(
+      <TestProvider>
+        <PointsInfoBottomSheet modalizeRef={mockModalizeRef} />
+      </TestProvider>,
+    );
+
+    expect(
+      getByText(
+        'Os seus pontos ganhos ficam disponíveis no saldo sempre que você pagar sua próxima fatura.',
+      ),
+    ).toBeTruthy();
+    expect(getByText('Entendi')).toBeTruthy();
+  });
+
+  it('should call close when "Entendi" button is pressed', () => {
+    const { getByText } = render(
+      <TestProvider>
+        <PointsInfoBottomSheet modalizeRef={mockModalizeRef} />
+      </TestProvider>,
+    );
+
+    const entendiButton = getByText('Entendi');
+    fireEvent.press(entendiButton);
+
+    expect(mockModalizeRef.current.close).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1);
+  });
+
+  it('should render with correct text content', () => {
+    const { getByText } = render(
+      <TestProvider>
+        <PointsInfoBottomSheet modalizeRef={mockModalizeRef} />
+      </TestProvider>,
+    );
+
+    const expectedText =
+      'Os seus pontos ganhos ficam disponíveis no saldo sempre que você pagar sua próxima fatura.';
+    expect(getByText(expectedText)).toBeTruthy();
+  });
+
+  it('should render button with correct text', () => {
+    const { getByText } = render(
+      <TestProvider>
+        <PointsInfoBottomSheet modalizeRef={mockModalizeRef} />
+      </TestProvider>,
+    );
+
+    expect(getByText('Entendi')).toBeTruthy();
+  });
+});

+describe('AirlineMilesMarketplace', () => {
+  const mockGoBack = jest.fn();
+  const mockNavigate = jest.fn();
+  const mockFetchToken = jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(true);
+  const mockReset = jest.fn();
+
+  const mockNavigation = {
+    goBack: mockGoBack,
+    navigate: mockNavigate,

+  const mockRoute = {

+
+  const defaultProps = {
+    navigation: mockNavigation,

+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    jest.clearAllMocks();
+    focusEffectCallback = null;
+  });
+
+  it('should render AnimationArrows when loading', () => {
+    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
+      redirectURL: '',

+      isError: false,
+      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,

+
+    const { UNSAFE_getByType } = render(
+      <TestProvider>
+        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
+      </TestProvider>,
+    );
+
+    expect(UNSAFE_getByType(AnimationArrows)).toBeTruthy();
+  });
+
+  it('should render ApiError when there is an error', () => {
+    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
+      redirectURL: '',
+      isLoading: false,
+      isError: true,
+      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,
+      reset: mockReset,
+    });
+
+    const { UNSAFE_getByType } = render(
+      <TestProvider>
+        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
+      </TestProvider>,
+    );
+
+    expect(UNSAFE_getByType(ApiError)).toBeTruthy();
+  });
+
+  it('should render ApiError when redirectURL is empty', () => {
+    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
+      redirectURL: '',
+      isLoading: false,
+      isError: false,
+      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,
+      reset: mockReset,
+    });
+
+    const { UNSAFE_getByType } = render(
+      <TestProvider>
+        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
+      </TestProvider>,
+    );
+
+    expect(UNSAFE_getByType(ApiError)).toBeTruthy();
+  });
+
+  it('should render WebView when redirectURL is available', () => {
+    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
+      redirectURL: 'https://example.com/marketplace',

+import { PropsModalizeRef } from '@semparar/design-system-app/src/@types/modelize';

+const props: MyOrdersItemPropsView = {
+  emptyImage: 'image.png',
+  filterGroups: [],
+  isError: false,
+  isLoading: false,
+  isRefreshing: false,
+  modalizeRefFilters: { current: null } as RefObject<PropsModalizeRef>,
+  myOrders: expectOrders,
+  handleGoBack: mockGoBack,
+  formatDate: formatDate,
+  handleOpenFilters: handleOpenFilters,
+  handleDetailsOrders: handleDetailsOrders,
+  handleRefresh: handleRefresh,
+  skeletonCount: SKELETON_COUNT,
+  screenWidth: 375,
+};
+
+jest.mock('@react-navigation/native', () => ({
+  useNavigation: () => ({
+    navigate: mockNavigate,
+    goBack: mockGoBack,
+  }),
+  useNavigationState: jest.fn(),
+}));
+
+jest.mock('react-native', () => {
+  const RN = jest.requireActual('react-native');
+  return {
+    ...RN,
+    FlatList: props => {
+      const { data = [], renderItem, ListEmptyComponent, testID } = props;
+
+      if (!data || data.length === 0) {
+        // render ListEmptyComponent
+        return (
+          <RN.View testID={testID}>
+            {ListEmptyComponent && <ListEmptyComponent />}
+          </RN.View>
+        );
+      }
+
+      return (
+        <RN.View testID={testID}>
+          {data.map((item, index) => {
+            props.keyExtractor?.(item, index);
+            return renderItem({
+              item,
+              index,
+            });
+          })}
+        </RN.View>
+      );
+    },
+  };
+});
+
+const renderComponentView = (props: MyOrdersItemPropsView) => {
+  return render(
+    <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
+      <MyOrdersCashbackView {...props} />
+    </ThemeProvider>,
+  );
+};
+
+describe('MyOrdersRewardProgram Component', () => {
+  it('should render InvoiceCashbackView with correct props', () => {
+    expect(renderComponentView(props)).toBeTruthy;
+  });
+
+  it('should render InvoiceCashbackView with empty orders', () => {

+      ...props,
+      myOrders: [],

+

+
+  it('should render InvoiceCashbackView loading list', () => {

+      ...props,
+      isLoading: true,
+    });
+
+    expect(getByTestId('loading-list-cashback')).toBeTruthy();
+
+    expect(getAllByTestId('loading-orders-cashback').length).toBe(
+      SKELETON_COUNT,
+    );
+  });
+
+  it('should render InvoiceCashbackView loading list', () => {
+    const { getByTestId } = renderComponentView({
+      ...props,
+      isError: true,
+    });
+
+    expect(getByTestId('orders-cashback-error').props.children).toBeTruthy();
+  });
+});

+import { PointsDetailProps } from '../../../src/screens/PointsDetail/Models';
+import {
+  getDetailScreenTitle,

+  getTagSubType,

+  getVehicleInfo,
+} from '../../../src/screens/PointsDetail/utils';
+
+describe('PointsDetail Utils', () => {
+  describe('getTransactionGainType', () => {
+    it('should return TAG when icon contains tag indicator', () => {
+      const transaction: PointsDetailProps['transaction'] = {
+        id: '1',
+        date: '2024-01-15',
+        description: 'Test',
+        type: 'RECEIVED',
+        amount: 100,
+        currencyValue: 10,
+        icon: 'icon-tag-test',
+        details: {
+          provider: '',
+          extraInfo: '',
+          fields: [],
+        },
+      };
+
+      expect(getTransactionGainType(transaction)).toBe('TAG');
+    });
+
+    it('should return TAG when description contains tag indicator', () => {
+      const transaction: PointsDetailProps['transaction'] = {
+        id: '1',
+        date: '2024-01-15',
+        description: 'Pedágio test',
+        type: 'RECEIVED',
+        amount: 100,
+        currencyValue: 10,
+        icon: 'icon-test',
+        details: {
+          provider: '',
+          extraInfo: '',
+          fields: [],
+        },
+      };
+
+      expect(getTransactionGainType(transaction)).toBe('TAG');
+    });
+
+    it('should return TAG when provider contains tag indicator', () => {
+      const transaction: PointsDetailProps['transaction'] = {
+        id: '1',
+        date: '2024-01-15',
+        description: 'Test',
+        type: 'RECEIVED',
+        amount: 100,
+        currencyValue: 10,
+        icon: 'icon-test',
+        details: {
+          provider: 'estacionamento',
+          extraInfo: '',
+          fields: [],
+        },
+      };
+
+      expect(getTransactionGainType(transaction)).toBe('TAG');

+
+    it('should return CASHBACK when provider contains cashback', () => {
+      const transaction: PointsDetailProps['transaction'] = {
+        id: '1',
+        date: '2024-01-15',
+        description: 'Cashback resgatado',

+    it('should return CASHBACK when provider contains cashback (case insensitive)', () => {

+        id: '1',

+

+
+    it('should return CARD when no tag indicators are found', () => {

+        id: '1',
+        date: '2024-01-15',

+        id: '1',

+        id: '1',

+        type: 'RECEIVED',

+
+  describe('getDetailScreenTitle', () => {

+      expect(getDetailScreenTitle('TAG')).toBe('Detalhes da pontuação');
+    });
+
+    it('should return "Detalhes de uso" for CASHBACK type with RESCUE transaction', () => {

+
+    it('should return "Detalhes do Cashback" for CASHBACK type when transaction type is not provided', () => {

+
+  describe('getVehicleInfo', () => {

+jest.mock('~/store', () => ({
+  useRootStore: Object.assign(jest.fn(), {
+    getState: jest.fn(),
+  }),
+}));
+
+jest.mock('@semparar/mf-app-user', () => ({
+  api: {
+    get: jest.fn(),
+  },
+}));
+
+import { api } from '@semparar/mf-app-user';
+import { airlineMilesApi } from '../../../src/services';
+
+describe('AirlineMilesApi', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    jest.clearAllMocks();
+  });
+
+  describe('getToken', () => {
+    it('should call correct endpoint with params', async () => {
+      const mockResponse = {
+        data: {
+          status: {
+            codigoRetorno: 0,
+            mensagem: 'Success',
+          },
+          dados: { redirectURL: 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc' },
+        },
+      };
+
+      (api.get as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(mockResponse);
+
+      const params = {
+        redirectTo: 'MARKETPLACE',
+        catalog: 'MILEAGE',
+      };
+      await airlineMilesApi.getToken(params);
+
+      expect(api.get).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+        '/marketplace/v1/vertem/getToken?redirectTo=MARKETPLACE&catalog=MILEAGE',
+      );
+    });
+
+    it('should return response data', async () => {
+      const mockData = {
+        status: {
+          codigoRetorno: 0,
+          mensagem: 'Success',
+        },
+        dados: { redirectURL: 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc' },
+      };
+
+      (api.get as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue({ data: mockData });
+
+      const params = {
+        redirectTo: 'MARKETPLACE',
+        catalog: 'MILEAGE',
+      };
+      const result = await airlineMilesApi.getToken(params);
+
+      expect(result).toEqual(mockData);
+    });
+
+    it('should handle API errors', async () => {
+      const mockError = new Error('Network Error');
+      (api.get as jest.Mock).mockRejectedValue(mockError);
+
+      const params = {
+        redirectTo: 'MARKETPLACE',
+        catalog: 'MILEAGE',
+      };
+
+      await expect(airlineMilesApi.getToken(params)).rejects.toThrow(
+        'Network Error',
+      );
+    });
+  });
+});
diff --git a/__tests__/services/airlineMiles/store.test.ts b/__tests__/services/airlineMiles/store.test.ts
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..1c49a6e1
--- /dev/null
+++ b/__tests__/services/airlineMiles/store.test.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,95 @@
+import {
+  AirlineMilesSlice,
+  createAirlineMilesSlice,
+} from '../../../src/services/store';
+
+describe('AirlineMilesSlice', () => {
+  let slice: AirlineMilesSlice;
+  let mockSet: jest.Mock;
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    mockSet = jest.fn();
+    slice = createAirlineMilesSlice(mockSet, jest.fn(), {} as any);
+  });
+
+  describe('initial state', () => {
+    it('should have correct initial values', () => {
+      expect(slice.airlineMiles.redirectURL).toBe('');
+      expect(slice.airlineMiles.isLoading).toBe(false);
+      expect(slice.airlineMiles.isError).toBe(false);
+      expect(slice.airlineMiles.errorMessage).toBe('');
+    });
+  });
+
+  describe('actions', () => {
+    it('should set loading state', () => {
+      slice.airlineMilesActions.setLoading(true);
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));
+
+      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
+      const newState = updateFn({ airlineMiles: slice.airlineMiles });
+
+      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isLoading).toBe(true);
+    });
+
+    it('should set redirectURL', () => {
+      const url = 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc';
+      slice.airlineMilesActions.setRedirectURL(url);
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));
+
+      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
+      const newState = updateFn({ airlineMiles: slice.airlineMiles });
+
+      expect(newState.airlineMiles.redirectURL).toBe(url);
+      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isLoading).toBe(false);
+      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isError).toBe(false);
+    });
+
+    it('should set error', () => {
+      const errorMessage = 'Failed to load';
+      slice.airlineMilesActions.setError(errorMessage);
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));
+
+      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
+      const newState = updateFn({ airlineMiles: slice.airlineMiles });
+
+      expect(newState.airlineMiles.errorMessage).toBe(errorMessage);
+      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isError).toBe(true);
+      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isLoading).toBe(false);
+    });
+
+    it('should clear error', () => {
+      slice.airlineMilesActions.clearError();
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));
+
+      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
+      const newState = updateFn({
+        airlineMiles: {
+          ...slice.airlineMiles,
+          isError: true,
+          errorMessage: 'some error',
+        },
+      });
+
+      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isError).toBe(false);
+      expect(newState.airlineMiles.errorMessage).toBe('');
+    });
+
+    it('should reset state', () => {
+      slice.airlineMilesActions.reset();
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
+        airlineMiles: {
+          redirectURL: '',
+          isLoading: false,
+          isError: false,
+          errorMessage: '',
+        },
+      });
+    });
+  });
+});
diff --git a/__tests__/services/airlineMiles/useAirlineMiles.test.tsx b/__tests__/services/airlineMiles/useAirlineMiles.test.tsx
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..2a210870
--- /dev/null
+++ b/__tests__/services/airlineMiles/useAirlineMiles.test.tsx
@@ -0,0 +1,153 @@
+import { act, renderHook } from '@testing-library/react-native';
+import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
+import { useAirlineMiles } from '../../../src/services';
+import { airlineMilesService } from '../../../src/services/services';
+
+jest.mock('~/store', () => ({
+  useRootStore: Object.assign(jest.fn(), {
+    getState: jest.fn(),
+  }),
+}));
+
+jest.mock('../../../src/services/services', () => ({
+  airlineMilesService: {
+    getToken: jest.fn(),
+  },
+}));
+
+jest.mock('zustand/shallow', () => ({
+  useShallow: (selector: any) => selector,
+}));
+
+describe('useAirlineMiles', () => {
+  const mockSetLoading = jest.fn();
+  const mockSetRedirectURL = jest.fn();
+  const mockSetError = jest.fn();
+  const mockReset = jest.fn();
+
+  const mockActions = {
+    setLoading: mockSetLoading,
+    setRedirectURL: mockSetRedirectURL,
+    setError: mockSetError,
+    reset: mockReset,
+  };
+
+  const mockState = {
+    redirectURL: '',
+    isLoading: false,
+    isError: false,
+    errorMessage: '',
+  };
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    jest.clearAllMocks();
+
+    (useRootStore as unknown as jest.Mock).mockImplementation((
+      selector: any,
+    ) => {
+      if (typeof selector === 'function') {
+        return selector({ airlineMiles: mockState });
+      }
+      return mockState;
+    });
+
+    (useRootStore.getState as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
+      airlineMilesActions: mockActions,
+    });
+  });
+
+  it('should return initial state', () => {
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());
+
+    expect(result.current.redirectURL).toBe('');
+    expect(result.current.isLoading).toBe(false);
+    expect(result.current.isError).toBe(false);
+  });
+
+  it('should fetch token successfully', async () => {
+    const mockResponse = {
+      status: {
+        codigoRetorno: 0,
+        mensagem: 'Success',
+      },
+      dados: { redirectURL: 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc' },
+    };
+
+    (airlineMilesService.getToken as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(mockResponse);
+
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());
+
+    let fetchResult: boolean = false;
+    await act(async () => {
+      fetchResult = await result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken();
+    });
+
+    expect(fetchResult).toBe(true);
+    expect(mockSetLoading).toHaveBeenCalledWith(true);
+    expect(mockSetRedirectURL).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      mockResponse.dados.redirectURL,
+    );
+  });
+
+  it('should handle API error response', async () => {
+    const mockResponse = {
+      status: {
+        codigoRetorno: 1,
+        mensagem: 'Token expired',
+      },
+      dados: { redirectURL: '' },
+    };
+
+    (airlineMilesService.getToken as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(mockResponse);
+
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());
+
+    let fetchResult: boolean = true;
+    await act(async () => {
+      fetchResult = await result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken();
+    });
+
+    expect(fetchResult).toBe(false);
+    expect(mockSetError).toHaveBeenCalledWith('Token expired');
+  });
+
+  it('should handle network error', async () => {
+    (airlineMilesService.getToken as jest.Mock).mockRejectedValue(
+      new Error('Network error'),
+    );
+
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());
+
+    let fetchResult: boolean = true;
+    await act(async () => {
+      fetchResult = await result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken();
+    });
+
+    expect(fetchResult).toBe(false);
+    expect(mockSetError).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      'Failed to load airline miles marketplace',
+    );
+  });
+
+  it('should call reset', () => {
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());
+
+    act(() => {
+      result.current.reset();
+    });
+
+    expect(mockReset).toHaveBeenCalled();
+  });
+
+  it('should have stable function references', () => {
+    const { result, rerender } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());
+
+    const initialFetch = result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken;
+    const initialReset = result.current.reset;
+
+    rerender({});
+
+    expect(result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken).toBe(initialFetch);
+    expect(result.current.reset).toBe(initialReset);
+  });
+});

+  });
+
+  describe('initial state', () => {
+    it('should have correct initial values', () => {
+      expect(slice.marketplace.status).toBe(MarketplaceStatus.IDLE);
+      expect(slice.marketplace.data).toBeNull();
+      expect(slice.marketplace.error).toBeUndefined();
+    });
+  });
+
+  describe('actions', () => {
+    it('should set status to loading', () => {
+      slice.marketplaceActions.setStatus(MarketplaceStatus.LOADING);
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));
+
+      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
+      const newState = updateFn({ marketplace: slice.marketplace });
+
+      expect(newState.marketplace.status).toBe(MarketplaceStatus.LOADING);
+      expect(newState.marketplace.error).toBeUndefined();
+    });
+
+    it('should set data', () => {
+      const mockData = {
+        dados: { redirectURL: 'https://marketplace.com' },
+        status: {
+          codigoRetorno: 0,
+          mensagem: 'Success',
+        },
+      };
+
+      slice.marketplaceActions.setData(mockData);
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));
+
+      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
+      const newState = updateFn({ marketplace: slice.marketplace });
+
+      expect(newState.marketplace.data).toEqual(mockData);
+      expect(newState.marketplace.status).toBe(MarketplaceStatus.SUCCESS);
+      expect(newState.marketplace.error).toBeUndefined();
+    });
+
+    it('should set error', () => {
+      const errorMessage = 'Failed to load marketplace';
+      slice.marketplaceActions.setError(errorMessage);
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));
+
+      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
+      const newState = updateFn({ marketplace: slice.marketplace });
+
+      expect(newState.marketplace.error).toBe(errorMessage);
+    });
+
+    it('should reset state', () => {
+      slice.marketplaceActions.reset();
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
+        marketplace: {
+          status: MarketplaceStatus.IDLE,
+          data: null,
+          error: undefined,
+        },
+      });
+    });
+  });
+});

diff --git a/__tests__/services/parameterPointsProgram/repository/index.test.ts b/__tests__/services/parameterPointsProgram/repository/index.test.ts

index 00000000..24625e87

@@ -0,0 +1,187 @@
+import type { ParameterPointsProgramResponse } from '../../../../src/services/models';
+import { mapParameterPointsProgramResponse } from '../../../../src/services/repository';
+
+describe('parameterPointsProgram Repository', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    jest.clearAllMocks();
+  });
+
+  it('should map API response and parse string values', () => {
+    const mockResponse: ParameterPointsProgramResponse[] = [
+      {
+        category: 'saiba mais',
+        value: JSON.stringify({
+          categories: [
+            {
+              category: 'Pontos',
+              questions: [
+                {
+                  question: 'Question 1',
+                  answer: 'Answer 1',
+                },
+              ],
+              order: 1,
+              icon: 'icon-benefits',
+            },
+            {
+              category: 'Pedidos',
+              questions: [
+                {
+                  question: 'Question 2',
+                  answer: 'Answer 2',
+                },
+              ],
+              order: 2,
+              icon: 'icon-payment-invoice',
+            },
+          ],
+        }),
+        dateCreated: '2024-01-01',
+        status: 1,
+        PK: 'pk1',
+        dateUpdated: '2024-01-01',
+      },
+    ];
+
+    const result = mapParameterPointsProgramResponse(mockResponse);
+
+    expect(result).toHaveLength(2);
+    expect(result[0].category).toBe('Pontos');
+    expect(result[0].value).toHaveProperty('icon', 'icon-benefits');
+    expect((result[0].value as any).order).toBe(1);
+    expect(result[1].category).toBe('Pedidos');
+    expect(result[1].value).toHaveProperty('icon', 'icon-payment-invoice');
+    expect((result[1].value as any).order).toBe(2);
+  });
+
+  it('should map categories with order values', () => {
+    const mockResponse: ParameterPointsProgramResponse[] = [
+      {
+        category: 'saiba mais',
+        value: JSON.stringify({
+          categories: [
+            {
+              category: 'Pontos',
+              questions: [],
+              order: 3,
+              icon: 'icon-benefits',
+            },
+            {
+              category: 'Pedidos',
+              questions: [],
+              order: 1,
+              icon: 'icon-payment-invoice',
+            },
+            {
+              category: 'Ajuda',
+              questions: [],
+              order: 2,
+              icon: 'icon-help',
+            },
+          ],
+        }),
+        dateCreated: '2024-01-01',
+        status: 1,
+        PK: 'pk1',
+        dateUpdated: '2024-01-01',
+      },
+    ];
+
+    const result = mapParameterPointsProgramResponse(mockResponse);
+
+    expect(result).toHaveLength(3);
+    // The function maps in order, doesn't sort
+    expect(result[0].category).toBe('Pontos');
+    expect((result[0].value as any).order).toBe(3);
+    expect(result[1].category).toBe('Pedidos');
+    expect((result[1].value as any).order).toBe(1);
+    expect(result[2].category).toBe('Ajuda');
+    expect((result[2].value as any).order).toBe(2);
+  });
+
+  it('should return empty array when saiba mais category not found', () => {
+    const mockResponse: ParameterPointsProgramResponse[] = [
+      {
+        category: 'Pontos',
+        value: {
+          questions: [],
+          order: 1,
+          icon: 'icon-benefits' as const,
+        },
+        dateCreated: '2024-01-01',
+        status: 1,
+        PK: 'pk1',
+        dateUpdated: '2024-01-01',
+      },
+    ];
+
+    const result = mapParameterPointsProgramResponse(mockResponse);
+
+    expect(result).toEqual([]);
+  });
+
+  it('should return empty array when value is not a string', () => {
+    const mockResponse: ParameterPointsProgramResponse[] = [
+      {
+        category: 'saiba mais',
+        value: {
+          questions: [],
+          order: 1,
+          icon: 'icon-benefits' as const,
+        },
+        dateCreated: '2024-01-01',
+        status: 1,
+        PK: 'pk1',
+        dateUpdated: '2024-01-01',
+      },
+    ];
+
+    const result = mapParameterPointsProgramResponse(mockResponse);
+
+    expect(result).toEqual([]);
+  });
+
+  it('should handle invalid JSON in value string', () => {
+    const mockResponse: ParameterPointsProgramResponse[] = [
+      {
+        category: 'saiba mais',
+        value: 'invalid json',
+        dateCreated: '2024-01-01',
+        status: 1,
+        PK: 'pk1',
+        dateUpdated: '2024-01-01',
+      },
+    ];
+
+    const result = mapParameterPointsProgramResponse(mockResponse);
+
+    expect(result).toEqual([]);
+  });
+
+  it('should return empty array when data is empty', () => {
+    const mockResponse: ParameterPointsProgramResponse[] = [];
+
+    const result = mapParameterPointsProgramResponse(mockResponse);
+
+    expect(result).toEqual([]);
+  });
+
+  it('should return empty array when categories is not an array', () => {
+    const mockResponse: ParameterPointsProgramResponse[] = [
+      {
+        category: 'saiba mais',
+        value: JSON.stringify({
+          categories: 'not an array',
+        }),
+        dateCreated: '2024-01-01',
+        status: 1,
+        PK: 'pk1',
+        dateUpdated: '2024-01-01',
+      },
+    ];
+
+    const result = mapParameterPointsProgramResponse(mockResponse);
+
+    expect(result).toEqual([]);
+  });
+});
diff --git a/__tests__/services/parameterPointsProgram/services/index.test.ts b/__tests__/services/parameterPointsProgram/services/index.test.ts

index 00000000..953378f4

@@ -0,0 +1,158 @@
+jest.mock('~/store', () => ({
+  useRootStore: {
+    getState: jest.fn(),
+  },
+}));
+jest.mock('../../../../src/services/api');
+jest.mock('../../../../src/services/repository');
+
+import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
+import { getParameterPointsProgramApi } from '../../../../src/services/api';
+import { ParameterPointsProgramStatus } from '../../../../src/services/models';
+import { mapParameterPointsProgramResponse } from '../../../../src/services/repository';
+import { getParameterPointsProgram } from '../../../../src/services/services';
+
+describe('parameterPointsProgram Service', () => {
+  const mockSetStatus = jest.fn();
+  const mockSetData = jest.fn();
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    jest.clearAllMocks();
+    (useRootStore.getState as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
+      parameterPointsProgram: {
+        status: ParameterPointsProgramStatus.IDLE,
+        data: undefined,
+        selectedCategory: undefined,
+      },
+      parameterPointsProgramActions: {
+        setStatus: mockSetStatus,
+        setData: mockSetData,
+        setSelectedCategory: jest.fn(),
+        reset: jest.fn(),
+      },
+    });
+  });
+
+  it('should fetch and set data on success', async () => {
+    const mockApiResponseData = [
+      {
+        category: 'Pontos',
+        value: {
+          questions: [
+            {
+              question: 'Question 1',
+              answer: 'Answer 1',
+            },
+          ],
+          order: 1,
+          icon: 'icon-benefits' as const,
+        },
+        dateCreated: '2024-01-01',
+        status: 1,
+        PK: 'pk1',
+        dateUpdated: '2024-01-01',
+      },
+    ];
+
+    const mockApiResponse = {
+      status: 200,
+      data: mockApiResponseData,
+    };
+
+    const mockMappedData = [
+      {
+        category: 'Pontos',
+        value: {
+          questions: [
+            {
+              question: 'Question 1',
+              answer: 'Answer 1',
+            },
+          ],
+          order: 1,
+          icon: 'icon-benefits' as const,
+        },
+        dateCreated: '2024-01-01',
+        status: 1,
+        PK: 'pk1',
+        dateUpdated: '2024-01-01',
+      },
+    ];
+
+    (getParameterPointsProgramApi as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(
+      mockApiResponse,
+    );
+    (mapParameterPointsProgramResponse as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue(
+      mockMappedData,
+    );
+
+    await getParameterPointsProgram();
+
+    expect(mockSetStatus).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
+    );
+    expect(getParameterPointsProgramApi).toHaveBeenCalled();
+    expect(mapParameterPointsProgramResponse).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      mockApiResponseData,
+    );
+    expect(mockSetData).toHaveBeenCalledWith(mockMappedData);
+    expect(mockSetStatus).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1);
+  });
+
+  it('should set error when API returns empty data array', async () => {
+    const mockApiResponse = {
+      status: 200,
+      data: [],
+    };
+
+    (getParameterPointsProgramApi as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(
+      mockApiResponse,
+    );
+
+    await getParameterPointsProgram();
+
+    expect(mockSetStatus).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
+    );
+    expect(mockSetStatus).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,
+    );
+    expect(mockSetData).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
+  });
+
+  it('should set error when API call fails', async () => {
+    const mockError = new Error('API Error');
+    (getParameterPointsProgramApi as jest.Mock).mockRejectedValue(mockError);
+
+    await getParameterPointsProgram();
+
+    expect(mockSetStatus).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
+    );
+    expect(mockSetStatus).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,
+    );
+    expect(mockSetData).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
+  });
+
+  it('should set error when status is not 200', async () => {
+    const mockApiResponse = {
+      status: 500,
+      data: [],
+    };
+
+    (getParameterPointsProgramApi as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(
+      mockApiResponse,
+    );
+
+    await getParameterPointsProgram();
+
+    expect(mockSetStatus).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
+    );
+    expect(mockSetStatus).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,
+    );
+    expect(mockSetData).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
+  });
+});

+import { ParameterPointsProgramStatus } from '../../../../src/services/models';
+import type { ParameterPointsProgramSlice } from '../../../../src/services/store';
+import { createParameterPointsProgramSlice } from '../../../../src/services/store';
+
+describe('parameterPointsProgram Store', () => {
+  let state: ParameterPointsProgramSlice;
+  let set: any;
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    state = {
+      parameterPointsProgram: {
+        status: ParameterPointsProgramStatus.IDLE,
+        data: undefined,
+        selectedCategory: undefined,
+        conversionFactor: 0.1,
+        pointsQuantity: 0,
+      },
+      parameterPointsProgramActions: {
+        setStatus: jest.fn(),
+        setData: jest.fn(),
+        setSelectedCategory: jest.fn(),
+        reset: jest.fn(),
+      },
+    };
+
+    set = jest.fn((updater: any) => {
+      if (typeof updater === 'function') {
+        const newState = updater(state);
+        Object.assign(state, newState);
+      } else {
+        Object.assign(state, updater);
+      }
+    });
+
+    const get = jest.fn(() => state);
+    const api = {};
+    const slice = createParameterPointsProgramSlice(set, get, api as any);
+    Object.assign(state, slice);
+  });
+
+  it('should have initial state', () => {
+    expect(state.parameterPointsProgram).toEqual({
+      status: ParameterPointsProgramStatus.IDLE,
+      data: undefined,
+      selectedCategory: undefined,
+      conversionFactor: 0.1,
+      pointsQuantity: 0,
+    });
+  });
+
+  it('should set loading state', () => {
+    state.parameterPointsProgramActions.setStatus(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
+    );
+
+    expect(state.parameterPointsProgram.status).toBe(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
+    );
+  });
+
+  it('should set data and set status to success', () => {
+    const mockData = [
+      {
+        category: 'Pontos',
+        value: {
+          questions: [
+            {
+              question: 'Question 1',
+              answer: 'Answer 1',
+            },
+          ],
+          order: 1,
+          icon: 'icon-benefits' as const,
+        },
+        dateCreated: '2024-01-01',
+        status: 1,
+        PK: 'pk1',
+        dateUpdated: '2024-01-01',
+      },
+    ];
+
+    state.parameterPointsProgramActions.setData(mockData);
+
+    expect(state.parameterPointsProgram.data).toEqual(mockData);
+    expect(state.parameterPointsProgram.status).toBe(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.SUCCESS,
+    );
+  });
+
+  it('should set error status', () => {
+    state.parameterPointsProgramActions.setStatus(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,
+    );
+
+    expect(state.parameterPointsProgram.status).toBe(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,
+    );
+  });
+
+  it('should set selected category', () => {
+    const mockCategory = {
+      category: 'Pontos',
+      value: {
+        questions: [],
+        order: 1,
+        icon: 'icon-benefits' as const,
+      },
+      dateCreated: '2024-01-01',
+      status: 1,
+      PK: 'pk1',
+      dateUpdated: '2024-01-01',
+    };
+
+    state.parameterPointsProgramActions.setSelectedCategory(mockCategory);
+
+    expect(state.parameterPointsProgram.selectedCategory).toEqual(mockCategory);
+  });
+
+  it('should reset to initial state', () => {
+    const mockData = [
+      {
+        category: 'Pontos',
+        value: {
+          questions: [
+            {
+              question: 'Question 1',
+              answer: 'Answer 1',
+            },
+          ],
+          order: 1,
+          icon: 'icon-benefits' as const,
+        },
+        dateCreated: '2024-01-01',
+        status: 1,
+        PK: 'pk1',
+        dateUpdated: '2024-01-01',
+      },
+    ];
+
+    state.parameterPointsProgramActions.setData(mockData);
+    state.parameterPointsProgramActions.setStatus(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,
+    );
+    state.parameterPointsProgramActions.reset();
+
+    expect(state.parameterPointsProgram).toEqual({
+      status: ParameterPointsProgramStatus.IDLE,
+      data: undefined,
+      selectedCategory: undefined,
+      conversionFactor: 0.1,
+      pointsQuantity: 0,
+    });
+  });
+
+  it('should handle multiple state updates', () => {
+    const mockData = [
+      {
+        category: 'Pontos',
+        value: {
+          questions: [
+            {
+              question: 'Question 1',
+              answer: 'Answer 1',
+            },
+          ],
+          order: 1,
+          icon: 'icon-benefits' as const,
+        },
+        dateCreated: '2024-01-01',
+        status: 1,
+        PK: 'pk1',
+        dateUpdated: '2024-01-01',
+      },
+    ];
+
+    state.parameterPointsProgramActions.setStatus(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
+    );
+    expect(state.parameterPointsProgram.status).toBe(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
+    );
+
+    state.parameterPointsProgramActions.setData(mockData);
+    expect(state.parameterPointsProgram.data).toEqual(mockData);
+    expect(state.parameterPointsProgram.status).toBe(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.SUCCESS,
+    );
+
+    state.parameterPointsProgramActions.setStatus(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,
+    );
+    expect(state.parameterPointsProgram.status).toBe(
+      ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,
+    );
+  });
+});

+import { PointsProductsStatus } from '../../../src/services/models';
+import {
+  createPointsProductsSlice,
+  PointsProductsSlice,
+} from '../../../src/services/store';
+
+describe('PointsProductsSlice', () => {
+  let slice: PointsProductsSlice;
+  let mockSet: jest.Mock;
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    mockSet = jest.fn();
+    slice = createPointsProductsSlice(mockSet, jest.fn(), {} as any);
+  });
+
+  describe('initial state', () => {
+    it('should have correct initial values', () => {
+      expect(slice.pointsProducts.products).toEqual([]);
+      expect(slice.pointsProducts.status).toBe(PointsProductsStatus.IDLE);
+      expect(slice.pointsProducts.error).toBeUndefined();
+    });
+  });
+
+  describe('actions', () => {
+    it('should set status to loading', () => {
+      slice.pointsProductsActions.setStatus(PointsProductsStatus.LOADING);
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));
+
+      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
+      const newState = updateFn({ pointsProducts: slice.pointsProducts });
+
+      expect(newState.pointsProducts.status).toBe(PointsProductsStatus.LOADING);
+      expect(newState.pointsProducts.error).toBeUndefined();
+    });
+
+    it('should set products', () => {
+      const mockProducts = [
+        {
+          name: 'Product 1',
+          productCode: 'P001',
+        },
+        {
+          name: 'Product 2',
+          productCode: 'P002',
+        },
+      ];
+
+      slice.pointsProductsActions.setProducts(mockProducts);
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));
+
+      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
+      const newState = updateFn({ pointsProducts: slice.pointsProducts });
+
+      expect(newState.pointsProducts.products).toEqual(mockProducts);
+      expect(newState.pointsProducts.status).toBe(PointsProductsStatus.SUCCESS);
+      expect(newState.pointsProducts.error).toBeUndefined();
+    });
+
+    it('should set error', () => {
+      const errorMessage = 'Failed to load products';
+      slice.pointsProductsActions.setError(errorMessage);
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));
+
+      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
+      const newState = updateFn({ pointsProducts: slice.pointsProducts });
+
+      expect(newState.pointsProducts.error).toBe(errorMessage);
+    });
+
+    it('should reset state', () => {
+      slice.pointsProductsActions.reset();
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
+        pointsProducts: {
+          products: [],
+          status: PointsProductsStatus.IDLE,
+          error: undefined,
+        },
+      });
+    });
+  });
+});

+  describe('initial state', () => {
+    it('should have correct initial values', () => {
+      expect(slice.rescue.status).toBe(RescueStatus.IDLE);
+      expect(slice.rescue.responseStatus).toBeUndefined();
+      expect(slice.rescue.error).toBeUndefined();
+    });
+  });
+
+  describe('actions', () => {
+    it('should set status to loading', () => {
+      slice.rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.LOADING);
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));
+
+      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
+      const newState = updateFn({ rescue: slice.rescue });
+
+      expect(newState.rescue.status).toBe(RescueStatus.LOADING);
+      expect(newState.rescue.error).toBeUndefined();
+    });
+
+    it('should set responseStatus', () => {
+      const responseStatus = { message: 'Success' };
+      slice.rescueActions.setResponseStatus(responseStatus);
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));
+
+      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
+      const newState = updateFn({ rescue: slice.rescue });
+
+      expect(newState.rescue.responseStatus).toEqual(responseStatus);
+    });
+
+    it('should set error', () => {
+      const errorMessage = 'Failed to rescue';
+      slice.rescueActions.setError(errorMessage);
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));
+
+      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
+      const newState = updateFn({ rescue: slice.rescue });
+
+      expect(newState.rescue.error).toBe(errorMessage);
+    });
+
+    it('should reset state', () => {
+      slice.rescueActions.reset();
+
+      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
+        rescue: {
+          status: RescueStatus.IDLE,
+          responseStatus: undefined,
+          error: undefined,
+        },
+      });
+    });
+  });
+});
