diff --git a/loop.ts b/loop.ts
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..0fd6e186
--- /dev/null
+++ b/loop.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,408 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bun
+
+/**
+ * Ralph Loop - Automated Claude Code Task Executor
+ *
+ * Based on Geoffrey Huntley's Ralph Wiggum technique:
+ * - Fresh context per iteration (new Claude instance each time)
+ * - Memory persists through files only (git, progress.txt)
+ * - One task per iteration
+ * - Completion signal terminates loop
+ *
+ * Uses Claude CLI's --output-format stream-json for real-time streaming.
+ *
+ * @see https://ghuntley.com/ralph/
+ */
+
+import { appendFileSync } from 'node:fs';
+import { chalk } from 'zx';
+
+function parseMaxIterations(arg: string | undefined): number {
+  const parsed = parseInt(arg ?? '100', 10);
+  if (isNaN(parsed) || parsed <= 0) {
+    console.error('Error: maxIterations must be a positive integer');
+    process.exit(1);
+  }
+  return parsed;
+}
+
+const CONFIG = Object.freeze({
+  maxIterations: parseMaxIterations(Bun.argv[2]),
+  delayBetweenIterations: 5000,
+  logFile: './loop.log',
+  progressFile: './.claude/progress.txt',
+  iterationTimeout: 600000, // 10 minutes max per iteration
+  // Dual-condition exit gate: requires explicit RALPH_STATUS with exit: true
+  exitPattern: /RALPH_STATUS:\s*\{[^}]*"exit":\s*true[^}]*\}/,
+});
+
+// Module-scope process reference for cleanup on signals
+let currentProc: ReturnType<typeof Bun.spawn> | null = null;
+
+const TASK_PROMPT = `Read \`./.claude/progress.txt\`, pick the most important pending task, execute it, update the file, commit, and stop.
+
+IMPORTANT RULES:
+- Do NOT change logic, only migrate structure
+- The source of truth is card-place, consult it only if in doubt
+- The code has already been copied to this repository
+- Everything must be migrated to the new architecture/Style Guide (./.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md)
+
+WORKFLOW:
+1. Read progress.txt and identify the most important pending single task
+2. If NO pending tasks exist, signal completion (see OUTPUT STATUS below) and stop
+3. If there is a task:
+   a. Update task status to "in progress" in progress.txt
+   b. Execute task following STYLE_GUIDE.md
+   c. Launch 3 review agents to deeply review the work, giving
+      the entire context of the task, simulating pull request.
+   d. Review output and consider improvements
+   e. Implement corrections if needed
+   f. Update progress.txt marking task as completed
+   g. Commit task changes (not include unrelated code)
+   h. Output status block (see below)
+   i. Stop
+
+OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
+You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:
+
+RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }
+
+Examples:
+- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
+- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }
+
+CRITICAL (THINK BEFORE ACTING):
+1. The FIRST thing you MUST do is write your understanding of each WORKFLOW statement
+2. The SECOND thing you MUST do is write your understanding of the work in progress described in progress.txt
+3. Only AFTER completing steps 1 and 2, proceed with the task execution
+4. If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
+5. Only set "exit": true when ALL tasks in progress.txt are completed
+6. For multiple lint/typescript/test errors, fix just one error and register others in progress.txt to be fixed in the next iteration
+7. For @semparar/* packages, read the real js code because some types are not updated correctly.
+`;
+
+function log(message: string, color?: string): void {
+  const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace('T', ' ').slice(0, 19);
+  const line = `[${timestamp}] ${message}`;
+  const coloredLine = color ? chalk[color](line) : line;
+
+  console.log(coloredLine);
+  appendFileSync(CONFIG.logFile, line + '\n');
+}
+
+async function checkDependencies(): Promise<void> {
+  const result = await Bun.$`which claude`.quiet().nothrow();
+  if (result.exitCode !== 0) {
+    log("ERROR: 'claude' command not found. Install Claude Code CLI.", 'red');
+    process.exit(1);
+  }
+
+  const progressFile = Bun.file(CONFIG.progressFile);
+  if (!(await progressFile.exists())) {
+    log(`ERROR: Progress file not found: ${CONFIG.progressFile}`, 'red');
+    process.exit(1);
+  }
+}
+
+function printHeader(): void {
+  console.log();
+  console.log(chalk.blue(`==========================================`));
+  console.log(chalk.blue(`   Ralph Loop - Bun.js Edition`));
+  console.log(chalk.blue(`==========================================`));
+  console.log(`Max iterations: ${chalk.yellow(CONFIG.maxIterations)}`);
+  console.log(`Log file: ${chalk.yellow(CONFIG.logFile)}`);
+  console.log(
+    `Delay between iterations: ${chalk.yellow(`${CONFIG.delayBetweenIterations / 1000}s`)}`,
+  );
+  console.log();
+}
+
+/**
+ * Stream-JSON message types from Claude Code CLI
+ * @see https://gist.github.com/SamSaffron/603648958a8c18ceae34939a8951d417
+ * @see https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/733
+ */
+interface ContentBlock {
+  type: 'text' | 'tool_use' | 'tool_result';
+  text?: string;
+  name?: string;
+  input?: unknown;
+  content?: string;
+}
+
+interface StreamMessage {
+  type: 'system' | 'assistant' | 'user' | 'result' | 'stream_event' | 'message';
+  subtype?: string;
+  role?: 'assistant' | 'user';
+  // For stream_event type
+  event?: {
+    type: string;
+    delta?: {
+      type: string;
+      text?: string;
+    };
+  };
+  // For result type
+  result?: string;
+  // For assistant/user/message types
+  message?: {
+    role?: string;
+    content?: ContentBlock[];
+  };
+  // Direct content array (alternative format)
+  content?: ContentBlock[];
+}
+
+async function runIteration(): Promise<{
+  output: string;
+  exitCode: number;
+}> {
+  log('Executing Claude Code...');
+
+  const proc = Bun.spawn(
+    [
+      'claude',
+      '-p',
+      TASK_PROMPT,
+      '--verbose',
+      '--debug',
+      '--dangerously-skip-permissions',
+      '--output-format',
+      'stream-json',
+      '--include-partial-messages',
+    ],
+    {
+      cwd: process.cwd(),
+      env: process.env,
+      stdout: 'pipe',
+      stderr: 'inherit', // Pass through stderr to console
+    },
+  );
+
+  // Store proc reference for cleanup on signals
+  currentProc = proc;
+
+  let output = '';
+  let timedOut = false;
+  const decoder = new TextDecoder();
+  let buffer = '';
+  let hasContentInBlock = false;
+
+  // Iteration timeout
+  const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => {
+    timedOut = true;
+    log('Iteration timeout reached. Killing process...', 'yellow');
+    try {
+      proc.kill();
+    } catch {
+      // Process may have already exited
+    }
+  }, CONFIG.iterationTimeout);
+
+  const reader = proc.stdout.getReader();
+
+  try {
+    while (true) {
+      const { done, value } = await reader.read();
+      if (done) break;
+
+      buffer += decoder.decode(value, { stream: true });
+
+      // Process complete JSON lines
+      const lines = buffer.split('\n');
+      buffer = lines.pop() || ''; // Keep incomplete line in buffer
+
+      for (const line of lines) {
+        if (!line.trim()) continue;
+
+        try {
+          const msg: StreamMessage = JSON.parse(line);
+
+          // 1. Handle stream_event (real-time token streaming)
+          if (msg.type === 'stream_event') {
+            if (msg.event?.type === 'content_block_start') {
+              hasContentInBlock = false;
+            }
+            if (msg.event?.type === 'content_block_delta') {
+              const text = msg.event.delta?.text;
+              if (text) {
+                process.stdout.write(text);
+                output += text;
+                hasContentInBlock = true;
+              }
+            }
+            if (msg.event?.type === 'content_block_stop' && hasContentInBlock) {
+              process.stdout.write('\n');
+              output += '\n';
+              hasContentInBlock = false;
+            }
+          }
+
+          // 2. Handle assistant/message types (full messages with content arrays)
+          // This captures sub-agent outputs and tool results
+          if (msg.type === 'assistant' || msg.type === 'message') {
+            const contentBlocks = msg.message?.content ?? msg.content;
+            if (contentBlocks && Array.isArray(contentBlocks)) {
+              for (const block of contentBlocks) {
+                if (block.type === 'text' && block.text) {
+                  process.stdout.write(block.text);
+                  output += block.text;
+                  process.stdout.write('\n');
+                  output += '\n';
+                }
+              }
+            }
+          }
+
+          // 3. Handle user messages (tool results)
+          if (msg.type === 'user') {
+            const contentBlocks = msg.message?.content ?? msg.content;
+            if (contentBlocks && Array.isArray(contentBlocks)) {
+              for (const block of contentBlocks) {
+                if (block.type === 'tool_result' && block.content) {
+                  // Tool results - print with prefix for visibility
+                  const resultText = `\n${chalk.reset.blackBright(block.content)}\n`;
+                  process.stdout.write(resultText);
+                  output += block.content;
+                  process.stdout.write('\n');
+                  output += '\n';
+                }
+              }
+            }
+          }
+
+          // 4. Handle result type - final output
+          if (msg.type === 'result' && msg.result) {
+            if (!output.trim()) {
+              output = msg.result;
+            }
+          }
+        } catch {
+          // Ignore JSON parse errors for incomplete/malformed lines
+        }
+      }
+    }
+
+    // Process any remaining buffer
+    if (buffer.trim()) {
+      try {
+        const msg: StreamMessage = JSON.parse(buffer);
+        if (msg.type === 'result' && msg.result) {
+          if (!output.trim()) output = msg.result;
+        }
+      } catch {
+        // Ignore
+      }
+    }
+  } finally {
+    clearTimeout(timeoutId);
+    reader.releaseLock();
+    currentProc = null;
+  }
+
+  if (timedOut) {
+    return {
+      output,
+      exitCode: 124,
+    }; // Standard timeout exit code
+  }
+
+  const exitCode = await proc.exited;
+  console.log(); // Newline after streaming output
+
+  return {
+    output,
+    exitCode,
+  };
+}
+
+async function main(): Promise<void> {
+  let cleanupCalled = false;
+
+  const cleanup = (signal: string) => {
+    if (cleanupCalled) return;
+    cleanupCalled = true;
+    console.log();
+    log(`Script interrupted by ${signal}. Cleaning up...`);
+
+    // Kill any running Claude process
+    if (currentProc) {
+      try {
+        currentProc.kill();
+      } catch {
+        // Process may have already exited
+      }
+    }
+
+    const exitCode = signal === 'SIGINT' ? 130 : 143;
+    process.exit(exitCode);
+  };
+
+  process.on('SIGINT', () => cleanup('SIGINT'));
+  process.on('SIGTERM', () => cleanup('SIGTERM'));
+
+  await checkDependencies();
+  printHeader();
+
+  log('Starting automated task loop...');
+
+  for (let iteration = 1; iteration <= CONFIG.maxIterations; iteration++) {
+    console.log();
+    console.log(chalk.green(`==========================================`));
+    log(`Iteration #${iteration} / ${CONFIG.maxIterations}`, 'green');
+    console.log(chalk.green(`==========================================`));
+    console.log();
+
+    const { output, exitCode } = await runIteration();
+
+    if (exitCode === 0) {
+      log('Claude Code execution completed successfully', 'green');
+    } else {
+      log(
+        `Claude Code exited with status ${exitCode}, checking output...`,
+        'yellow',
+      );
+    }
+
+    // Dual-condition exit gate: requires explicit RALPH_STATUS with exit: true
+    const hasExitSignal = CONFIG.exitPattern.test(output);
+    if (hasExitSignal) {
+      // Extract status for logging
+      const match = output.match(/RALPH_STATUS:\s*(\{[^}]*})/);
+      const status = match ? match[1] : 'unknown';
+      console.log();
+      console.log(chalk.green(`==========================================`));
+      log('ALL TASKS COMPLETED!', 'green');
+      log(`Exit status: ${status}`, 'green');
+      log(`Total iterations: ${iteration}`, 'green');
+      console.log(chalk.green(`==========================================`));
+      process.exit(0);
+    }
+
+    // More specific error detection - avoid false positives from "error handling", etc.
+    const hasErrors =
+      exitCode !== 0 ||
+      /(?:^|\n)\s*(?:Error:|ERROR:|Failed:|FAILED:)/m.test(output) ||
+      output.includes('claude: error');
+    if (hasErrors) {
+      log('Warning: Potential errors detected in output.', 'yellow');
+    }
+
+    if (iteration < CONFIG.maxIterations) {
+      console.log();
+      log(
+        `Waiting ${CONFIG.delayBetweenIterations / 1000} seconds before next iteration...`,
+      );
+      await Bun.sleep(CONFIG.delayBetweenIterations);
+    }
+  }
+
+  console.log();
+  console.log(chalk.red(`==========================================`));
+  log(`MAX ITERATIONS REACHED (${CONFIG.maxIterations})`, 'red');
+  log('Loop stopped - tasks may still be pending', 'red');
+  console.log(chalk.red(`==========================================`));
+  process.exit(1);
+}
+
+main();

new file mode 100644
index 00000000..d31fdead
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scripts/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,51 @@
+# Scripts
+
+Este diretório contém um conjunto de utilitários desenvolvidos para melhorar a integridade do projeto e detectar erros, problemas de performance e código desatualizado durante o desenvolvimento.
+
+## Principais Scripts
+
+### `tscheck` (`typescript-check.ts`)
+
+Como consumimos arquivos `.ts` e `tsx` (não buildado) diretamente do `node_modules`, o tsc tentava validar estes arquivos,
+o que gerava muitos erros, impossibilitando habilitar a checagem de tipos.
+Este script faz a análise de erros apenas no código-fonte local, tornando a checagem de tipo viável.
+
+[O typescript não permite ignorar checagem de arquivos .ts, apenas dos `.d.ts`](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/41883#issuecomment-1758692340).
+
+**Verificação de Pacotes Desatualizados**: O script também compara as versões dos pacotes internos do escopo `@semparar/*` com as versões utilizadas no projeto "Super App". Caso uma dependência local esteja desatualizada, um aviso será exibido com o comando para atualização, ajudando a prevenir _breaking changes_ por uso incorreto destes.
+
+### `sync-typings` (`sync-typings.ts`)
+
+Este comando automatiza a sincronização de tipos com o projeto principal (Super App).
+
+- **Geração de Tipos**: O script (`sync-typings.ts`) escaneia todos os imports que começam com `~/` no código-fonte - `~/` é path `alias` interceptado no Super App.
+- **Criação de Declarações**: Ele então gera automaticamente os arquivos de declaração de tipos (`.d.ts`) - ignorando os erros de tipo nestes arquivos -
+  correspondentes a esses imports, salvando-os em `@types/super-app.d.ts`. Isso permite usar código do Super App sem a necessidade de copiar e colar e atualizar arquivos manualmente.
+  > ⚠️ É importante garantir que ao executar este script, o projeto do super app esteja atualizado e localizado em `../appSemPararv3`
+
+### Arquivos
+
+- `config.ts`
+  - Centraliza as configurações e constantes usadas pelos scripts, como caminhos de diretórios (`CWD`, `MICRO_FRONTEND_SRC`, `SUPER_APP_PATH`) e o `package.json` do projeto.
+
+- `sync-typings.ts`
+  - Orquestra o processo de geração de tipos. Ele utiliza o `DiscoveryUsedImports` para encontrar os imports do Super App (`~/`), invoca o `Generator` para criar as declarações e o `PackageVersionCheck` para validar as dependências.
+
+- `generate-types.ts`
+  - É o motor principal por trás da geração de tipos. Utiliza a API do Compilador TypeScript (`ts.Program`, `ts.TypeChecker`) para analisar arquivos de código-fonte, extrair tipos de `interfaces`, `types`, `consts` e funções exportadas, e gerar um único arquivo de declaração (`.d.ts`) com módulos `declare module '...'`.
+
+- `typescript-check.ts`
+  - Implementa a lógica customizada para a verificação de tipos. Ele invoca a API do TypeScript para obter todos os diagnósticos de erros e os filtra para reportar apenas aqueles relevantes para o código-fonte do projeto atual.
+
+- `utils.ts`
+  - Fornece um conjunto de funções auxiliares reutilizáveis pelos outros scripts, como:
+    - `DiscoveryUsedImports`: Para encontrar imports que correspondem a um padrão.
+    - `PackageVersionCheck`: Para comparar versões de pacotes.
+    - `logSync`: Para escrita síncrona no terminal.
+    - Funções de utilidade geral (`invariant`, `createMatcher`, etc.).
+
+- `formatter.ts`
+  - Um wrapper simples para o `prettier`. É utilizado pelo `sync-typings.ts` para formatar o código de declaração de tipos gerado antes de salvá-lo em disco.
+
+- `eslint-formatter-sorted.js`
+  - Um formatador para o ESLint que exibe de forma agrupada os resultados.

+import path from 'path';
+import { fs } from 'zx';
+
+export namespace ScriptConfig {
+  export const CWD = process.cwd();
+  export const MICRO_FRONTEND_SRC = path.resolve(CWD, 'src');
+  export const SUPER_APP_PATH = path.resolve(CWD, '../appSemPararv3');
+
+  export type PackageJSON = Readonly<{
+    name: string;
+    version: string;
+    scripts: Record<string, string>;
+    dependencies: Record<string, string>;
+    devDependencies: Record<string, string>;
+    [K: string]: unknown;
+  }>;
+
+  export const packageJSON: PackageJSON = fs.readJSONSync(
+    path.join(CWD, './package.json'),
+    'utf8',
+  );
+
+  export const syncSuperAppTypesConfig = {
+    folder: MICRO_FRONTEND_SRC,
+    match: ['~/**'],
+    ignore: [
+      '~/redux/store/createReducer', // broken
+      '~/routes/navigation', // broken
+      '~/store', // we use ./store.d.ts
+    ],
+  };
+}

+/* istanbul ignore file */
+/**
+ * Format eslint results listing by severity
+ * @param results
+ * @returns {string}
+ */
+const chalk = require('chalk');
+
+module.exports = function (results) {
+  const warnings = [];
+  const errors = [];
+
+  results.forEach(result => {
+    const { filePath, messages } = result;
+    messages.forEach(message => {
+      const line = message.line || 0;
+      const column = message.column || 0;
+      const entry = {
+        filePath,
+        line,
+        column,
+        ruleId: message.ruleId,
+        message: message.message,
+      };
+
+      if (message.severity === 1) {
+        // severity 1 === 'warn'
+        warnings.push(entry);
+      } else if (message.severity === 2) {
+        // severity 2 === 'error'
+        errors.push(entry);
+      }
+    });
+  });
+
+  let output = '';
+  let totalProblems = 0;
+
+  if (warnings.length > 0) {
+    output += `\n${chalk.yellow('[ESLINT WARNINGS]')}\n`;
+    warnings.forEach(w => {
+      output += `  ${chalk.yellow('Warning')}: ${w.message} (${w.ruleId})\n`;
+      output += `  ${chalk.gray(`at ${w.filePath}:${w.line}:${w.column}`)}\n\n`;
+    });
+    totalProblems += warnings.length;
+  }
+
+  if (errors.length > 0) {
+    output += `\n${chalk.red('[ESLINT ERRORS]')}\n`;
+    errors.forEach(e => {
+      output += `  ${chalk.red('Error')}: ${e.message} (${e.ruleId})\n`;
+      output += `  ${chalk.gray(`at ${e.filePath}:${e.line}:${e.column}`)}\n\n`;
+    });
+    totalProblems += errors.length;
+  }
+
+  if (totalProblems > 0) {
+    output += `\n${chalk.bold.red(`✖ ${totalProblems} problems (${errors.length} errors, ${warnings.length} warnings)`)}\n`;
+  } else {
+    output += `\n${chalk.bold.green('✔ No problems found!')}\n`;
+  }
+
+  return output;
+};

+import * as prettier from 'prettier';
+
+export namespace Formatter {
+  export function Format(code: string) {
+    return prettier.format(code, {
+      parser: 'typescript',
+      printWidth: 80,
+      semi: true,
+      singleQuote: true,
+      trailingComma: 'all',
+    });
+  }
+}
diff --git a/scripts/core/generate-types.ts b/scripts/core/generate-types.ts
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..b10ffef7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scripts/core/generate-types.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,645 @@
+import path from 'path';
+import ts from 'typescript';
+import {
+  escapeRegExp,
+  hashString,
+  invariant,
+  lastElement,
+  logSync,
+} from './utils';
+
+import { chalk, fs } from 'zx';
+
+const DEFAULT_MAX_DEPTH = 4;
+
+/**
+ * This script takes a list of typescript file paths and bundle a single,
+ * unified string of typescript module declarations.
+
+ * Example of generated module declarations:
+ *
+ * declare module '~/settings/environment' {
+ *   export default {} as { type: string; semParar: { ... }}
+ * }
+ *
+ * declare module '~/hooks/tracking' {
+ *   export declare const foo: 'bar'
+ * }
+ *
+ * @see: Wiki - Using the Compiler API:
+ * https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/wiki/Using-the-Compiler-API
+ */
+export namespace Generator {
+  let CWD: string;
+  let PROJECT_PATH: string;
+  let PROGRAM: ts.Program;
+  let CHECKER: ts.TypeChecker;
+  let HOST: ts.CompilerHost;
+  let MAX_DEPTH: number;
+  let PACKAGE_NAME: string | undefined;
+  let PACKAGE_NAMESPACE: string | undefined;
+  let TRANSFORM_MODULE: Params['transform'] | null;
+  let MODULES_PARAM: string[];
+
+  // keep track of visited files to avoid infinite loops in case
+  // of circular dependencies.
+  const VISITED = new Set<string>();
+  const MODULE_INFO_MAP = new Map<string, ModuleInfo>();
+  const ERRORS = new Set<string>();
+
+  // describes a generated module
+  export type ResultingModuleDeclaration = {
+    body: string;
+  } & ModuleInfo;
+
+  export type ModuleInfo = {
+    fileName: string; // source file path
+    sourceFile: ts.SourceFile;
+    destinationSpecifier: string;
+    pathFromNodeModules: string | null;
+    genericSpecifier: string;
+    isNodeModule: boolean;
+    packageName: string;
+    isSameNpmScope: boolean;
+    ignore: boolean;
+  };
+
+  // An imported value
+  export type Imported = {
+    specifier: string;
+    node: ts.Node;
+    fileName: string;
+    destinationSpecifier: string;
+    symbol: string;
+    isDefaultImport: boolean;
+  };
+
+  // Convert inline `import('...').Thing` types into standard imports and
+  // normalize the imported paths.
+  function ExtractInlineImports(params: {
+    parentFileName: string;
+    code: string;
+  }) {
+    const { parentFileName, code } = params;
+
+    const tempSourceFile = ts.createSourceFile(
+      'temp.ts',
+      code,
+      ts.ScriptTarget.Latest,
+      true,
+    );
+
+    const imports = new Map<string, Map<string, Imported>>();
+
+    let transformedBody = code;
+
+    function visit(node: ts.Node) {
+      // searching for `import('some-path')` nodes.
+      if (ts.isImportTypeNode(node)) {
+        const argumentNode = node.argument;
+
+        if (
+          ts.isLiteralTypeNode(argumentNode) &&
+          ts.isStringLiteral(argumentNode.literal)
+        ) {
+          const specifier = argumentNode.literal.text;
+          const { fileName, destinationSpecifier } = ParseModuleInfo(
+            specifier,
+            parentFileName,
+          );
+
+          // get the type name, e.g., 'MyType' in `import('...').MyType`.
+          const typeName = node.qualifier?.getText(tempSourceFile);
+
+          // create a safe fallback name if it's a default import.
+          const safeTypeName =
+            typeName && typeName !== 'default'
+              ? typeName
+              : `T_${hashString(fileName)}`;
+
+          const imported: Imported = {
+            fileName,
+            isDefaultImport: !typeName,
+            node,
+            specifier,
+            symbol: safeTypeName.split('.')[0],
+            destinationSpecifier,
+          };
+
+          const list = imports.get(fileName) ?? new Map();
+          list.set(imported.symbol, imported);
+          imports.set(fileName, list);
+
+          // replace the inline import() with just the type name.
+          let regexText = `\\bimport\\(([^)]+)\\)(\\.${escapeRegExp(safeTypeName)})?\\b`;
+          const regex = new RegExp(regexText, 'g');
+          transformedBody = transformedBody.replace(regex, safeTypeName);
+        } else {
+          console.warn(
+            `Unhandled import node type: ${node.getText(tempSourceFile)}`,
+          );
+        }
+      }
+      ts.forEachChild(node, visit);
+    }
+
+    visit(tempSourceFile);
+
+    return {
+      transformedBody,
+      imports,
+    };
+  }
+
+  // Used to extract types from a source file.
+  function FileTypesExtractor(
+    sourceFile: ts.SourceFile,
+    moduleSymbol: ts.Symbol,
+  ) {
+    const processed = new Set<ts.Symbol>(); // to avoid processing the same symbol twice.
+
+    const bodyPartBySymbol = new Map<ts.Symbol, string>();
+
+    // A typescript `symbol` represents a named declaration like a variable or
+    // interface. Here we recursively scan and extract types as a strings.
+    function scan(symbol: ts.Symbol) {
+      if (!symbol) return;
+      if (processed.has(symbol)) return;
+      processed.add(symbol);
+
+      const declaration =
+        symbol.valueDeclaration ?? // things that have a "value" at runtime (vars, functions).
+        symbol.declarations?.[0]; // things that are just types (interfaces, type aliases).
+
+      if (!declaration) return;
+
+      if (ts.isImportSpecifier(declaration)) return;
+
+      // scanning types that are used but not exported
+      ts.forEachChild(declaration, function visit(node) {
+        if (ts.isTypeReferenceNode(node)) {
+          const refSymbol = CHECKER.getSymbolAtLocation(node.typeName);
+
+          if (refSymbol?.declarations) {
+            const refDecl = refSymbol.declarations[0];
+            const definedAtSameFile =
+              refDecl.getSourceFile().fileName === sourceFile.fileName;
+            // if the referenced type is in the same file,
+            // we need to scan it too.
+            if (definedAtSameFile) {
+              scan(refSymbol);
+            }
+          }
+        }
+        ts.forEachChild(node, visit);
+      });
+
+      if (bodyPartBySymbol.has(symbol)) return;
+
+      if (
+        ts.isInterfaceDeclaration(declaration) ||
+        ts.isTypeAliasDeclaration(declaration) ||
+        ts.isEnumDeclaration(declaration) ||
+        ts.isClassDeclaration(declaration)
+      ) {
+        // for interfaces, types, enums, and classes,
+        // we can just get their original text.
+        bodyPartBySymbol.set(symbol, declaration.getText());
+        return;
+      }
+
+      // for variables and functions, we use the TypeChecker to get
+      // the full type signature.
+      const type = CHECKER.getTypeOfSymbolAtLocation(symbol, declaration);
+      const typeText = CHECKER.typeToString(
+        type,
+        declaration,
+        ts.TypeFormatFlags.NoTruncation |
+          ts.TypeFormatFlags.UseFullyQualifiedType,
+      );
+
+      if (typeText === 'any') return;
+
+      if (symbol.getName() === 'default') {
+        // handle `export default ...`
+        bodyPartBySymbol.set(symbol, `export default {} as ${typeText};`);
+        return;
+      }
+
+      // handle `export const foo = ...`
+      bodyPartBySymbol.set(
+        symbol,
+        `export declare const ${symbol.getName()}: ${typeText};`,
+      );
+    }
+
+    CHECKER.getExportsOfModule(moduleSymbol).forEach(scan);
+
+    let { imports, transformedBody } = ExtractInlineImports({
+      parentFileName: sourceFile.fileName,
+      code: [...bodyPartBySymbol.values()].join('\n'),
+    });
+
+    ts.forEachChild(sourceFile, node => {
+      if (ts.isImportDeclaration(node)) {
+        ParseImportDeclaration(sourceFile, node).forEach(imported => {
+          const list = imports.get(imported.fileName) ?? new Map();
+          list.set(imported.symbol, imported);
+          imports.set(imported.fileName, list);
+        });
+      }
+    });
+
+    return {
+      imports,
+      body: transformedBody,
+    };
+  }
+
+  function isIdentifierOfAType(identifier: ts.Identifier) {
+    const symbol = CHECKER.getSymbolAtLocation(identifier);
+    if (!symbol) return false;
+    const flags = CHECKER.getAliasedSymbol(symbol).getFlags();
+    return flags & ts.SymbolFlags.Type && !(flags & ts.SymbolFlags.Value);
+  }
+
+  function ParseImportDeclaration(
+    sourceFile: ts.SourceFile,
+    declaration: ts.ImportDeclaration,
+  ): Imported[] {
+    const namedBindings = declaration.importClause?.namedBindings;
+
+    const importedSymbols: string[] = [];
+
+    const defaultImportedName = declaration.importClause?.name?.text;
+
+    if (
+      declaration.importClause?.name &&
+      isIdentifierOfAType(declaration.importClause.name)
+    ) {
+      importedSymbols.push(declaration.importClause.name.text);
+    }
+
+    if (namedBindings && ts.isNamedImports(namedBindings)) {
+      namedBindings.elements.forEach(element => {
+        if (!isIdentifierOfAType(element.name)) return;
+        const name = element.name.text;
+        importedSymbols.push(name);
+      });
+    }
+
+    const specifier = declaration.moduleSpecifier
+      .getText(sourceFile)
+      .slice(1, -1);
+
+    const { fileName, destinationSpecifier } = ParseModuleInfo(
+      specifier,
+      sourceFile.fileName,
+    );
+
+    return importedSymbols.map((symbol): Imported => {
+      return {
+        symbol,
+        fileName,
+        destinationSpecifier,
+        specifier,
+        node: declaration,
+        isDefaultImport: symbol === defaultImportedName,
+      };
+    });
+  }
+
+  // resolve file locations, path alias, etc
+  function ParseModuleInfo(specifier: string, fromPath: string): ModuleInfo {
+    const fileName =
+      ts.resolveModuleName(
+        specifier,
+        fromPath,
+        PROGRAM.getCompilerOptions(),
+        HOST,
+      ).resolvedModule?.resolvedFileName ?? '';
+
+    if (!fileName) {
+      logSync(`Failed to resolve "${specifier}" from ${fromPath}.`, 'stderr');
+    }
+
+    const isNodeModule = fileName.includes('/node_modules/');
+    let packageName = '';
+
+    const pathFromNodeModules = (() => {
+      if (!isNodeModule) return null;
+
+      let nodeModulePath = normalizePath(fileName.split('/node_modules/')[1]);
+
+      packageName = nodeModulePath
+        .split('/')
+        .slice(
+          0,
+          // for scoped packages (@scope/package_name), we extract 2 parts
+          nodeModulePath.startsWith('@') ? 2 : 1,
+        )
+        .join('/');
+
+      return nodeModulePath;
+    })();
+
+    const genericSpecifier = normalizePath(
+      (() => {
+        const isProjectFile = fileName.startsWith(PROJECT_PATH);
+        const isLocalFile = !isProjectFile && fileName.startsWith(CWD);
+
+        const scope = isLocalFile ? '@generated_local' : '@generated';
+
+        if (!isLocalFile && !isProjectFile) {
+          return path.join(
+            scope,
+            // masking the file path to not include the
+            // full path (with username, etc)
+            hashString(path.dirname(fileName)),
+            path.basename(fileName),
+          );
+        }
+
+        const resultPath = lastElement(
+          isProjectFile ? fileName.split(PROJECT_PATH) : fileName.split(CWD),
+        );
+
+        return path.join(scope, resultPath);
+      })(),
+    );
+
+    const isSameNpmScope = Boolean(
+      packageName &&
+      PACKAGE_NAMESPACE &&
+      packageName.startsWith(PACKAGE_NAMESPACE),
+    );
+
+    let destinationSpecifier = pathFromNodeModules ?? genericSpecifier;
+
+    if (MODULES_PARAM.includes(specifier)) {
+      destinationSpecifier = specifier;
+    }
+
+    let partial: Omit<ModuleInfo, 'sourceFile'> = {
+      pathFromNodeModules,
+      genericSpecifier,
+      destinationSpecifier,
+      fileName,
+      isNodeModule,
+      packageName,
+      isSameNpmScope,
+      ignore: false,
+    };
+
+    let moduleInfo = Object.defineProperties(partial, {
+      sourceFile: {
+        get() {
+          const sourceFile = PROGRAM.getSourceFile(fileName);
+          invariant(sourceFile, `Failed to resolve ${fileName}`);
+          return sourceFile;
+        },
+      },
+    }) as ModuleInfo;
+
+    if (TRANSFORM_MODULE && !VISITED.has(fileName)) {
+      const transformed = TRANSFORM_MODULE(moduleInfo);
+      if (transformed === null) {
+        moduleInfo.ignore = true;
+      } else {
+        moduleInfo = transformed;
+      }
+    }
+
+    const cachedData = MODULE_INFO_MAP.get(fileName);
+    const oldId = cachedData?.destinationSpecifier;
+    const newId = moduleInfo.destinationSpecifier;
+    if (oldId && oldId !== newId) {
+      // if the same module is imported with different identifiers,
+      // we use the first one.
+      return cachedData;
+    }
+    MODULE_INFO_MAP.set(fileName, moduleInfo);
+    return moduleInfo;
+  }
+
+  /**
+   * The entry/recursive part - it visits a module file, extracts its types,
+   * and then recursively visits all of its local imports.
+   * @param specifier Module import string (e.g., '~/components/Button').
+   * @param parentFilePath File that is importing this module.
+   * @param level The current recursion depth.
+   */
+  function* Visit(
+    specifier: string,
+    parentFilePath: string,
+    level: number,
+  ): Generator<ResultingModuleDeclaration> {
+    try {
+      if (level > MAX_DEPTH) return;
+
+      // real file path on disk.
+      const moduleInfo = ParseModuleInfo(specifier, parentFilePath);
+      if (moduleInfo.ignore) return;
+
+      const { fileName, sourceFile } = moduleInfo;
+
+      // check for already processed this exact import path.
+      if (VISITED.has(fileName)) return;
+      VISITED.add(fileName);
+
+      const moduleSymbol = CHECKER.getSymbolAtLocation(sourceFile);
+      if (!moduleSymbol) return;
+
+      logSync(
+        chalk.gray(
+          `‣ processing ${path.relative(PROJECT_PATH, sourceFile.fileName)}`,
+        ),
+      );
+
+      // extractor to get all the type information from the file.
+      const file = FileTypesExtractor(sourceFile, moduleSymbol);
+
+      // recursively visit all the used imports in this file.
+      for (let [importedModulePath, imported] of file.imports) {
+        const head: string[] = [];
+
+        imported.forEach(el => {
+          const { symbol, destinationSpecifier } = el;
+
+          if (symbol === 'Omit' && destinationSpecifier === 'react-redux') {
+            // hotfix importing Omit from 'react-redux'
+            return;
+          }
+
+          const regex = new RegExp(`\\b${escapeRegExp(el.symbol)}\\b`);
+          if (!regex.test(file.body)) return;
+
+          if (el.isDefaultImport) {
+            let txt = head[0] ?? '';
+            txt += `import ${el.symbol} from '${el.specifier}';\n`;
+            head[0] = txt;
+          } else {
+            let txt = head[1] ?? '';
+            let first = !txt;
+            if (first) txt = `import {`;
+            txt += first ? el.symbol : `,${el.symbol}`;
+            head[1] = txt;
+          }
+        });
+
+        let txt = head[1] ?? '';
+        if (txt) {
+          txt += `} from '${imported.values().next().value!.destinationSpecifier}';\n`;
+        }
+
+        yield* Visit(importedModulePath, sourceFile.fileName, level + 1);
+
+        file.body = `${txt} ${file.body}\n`;
+      }
+
+      yield {
+        ...file,
+        ...moduleInfo,
+        destinationSpecifier:
+          level === 0 ? specifier : moduleInfo.destinationSpecifier,
+      };
+    } catch (e: any) {
+      logSync(`  🐞 ${e.message}`, 'warning');
+      ERRORS.add(lastElement(specifier.split('/node_modules/')));
+      return;
+    }
+  }
+
+  // Return a typescript `Program` (Compiler)
+  function SetupCompiler() {
+    const configPath = ts.findConfigFile(
+      PROJECT_PATH,
+      ts.sys.fileExists,
+      'tsconfig.json',
+    );
+
+    invariant(
+      configPath,
+      `Could not find a valid tsconfig.json at ${PROJECT_PATH}`,
+    );
+
+    const configFile = ts.readConfigFile(configPath, ts.sys.readFile);
+
+    const compilerOptions = {
+      ...configFile.config.compilerOptions,
+      noEmitOnError: false,
+    };
+
+    const parsedConfig = ts.parseJsonConfigFileContent(
+      {
+        ...configFile.config,
+        compilerOptions,
+      },
+      ts.sys,
+      PROJECT_PATH,
+    );
+
+    HOST = ts.createCompilerHost(parsedConfig.options);
+    PROGRAM = ts.createProgram(
+      parsedConfig.fileNames,
+      parsedConfig.options,
+      HOST,
+    );
+    CHECKER = PROGRAM.getTypeChecker();
+  }
+
+  export type Params = {
+    // the root directory of the project to generate typings from.
+    projectPath: string;
+    // an array of entry-point module paths to start the generation from.
+    modules: string[];
+    // how many levels of imports deep should the generator go.
+    maxDepth?: number;
+    // used to transform the final module before it's rendered.
+    // returning null will skip this module from output
+    transform?: (path: ModuleInfo) => ModuleInfo | null;
+    // if true, code from node_modules will not be bundled
+    // defaults to true
+    ignoreNoduleModules?: boolean;
+    // defaults to process.cwd()
+    cwd?: string;
+  };
+
+  export function Generate(params: Params) {
+    const { modules } = params;
+
+    // defining internal configuration
+    MODULES_PARAM = modules;
+    VISITED.clear();
+    MODULE_INFO_MAP.clear();
+    ERRORS.clear();
+    CWD = params.cwd ?? process.cwd();
+    PROJECT_PATH = params.projectPath;
+    MAX_DEPTH = params.maxDepth ?? DEFAULT_MAX_DEPTH;
+    TRANSFORM_MODULE = params.transform ?? null;
+
+    PACKAGE_NAME = (() => {
+      const jsonPath = path.resolve(CWD, 'package.json');
+      if (!fs.existsSync(jsonPath)) {
+        return logSync(`No package.json found at current working directory.`);
+      }
+      const packageJSON = fs.readJSONSync(jsonPath);
+      return packageJSON.name;
+    })();
+
+    PACKAGE_NAMESPACE = (() => {
+      if (!PACKAGE_NAME) return;
+      const parts = PACKAGE_NAME.split('/');
+      if (parts.length === 2) return parts[0];
+    })();
+
+    // start the typescript compiler API.
+    SetupCompiler();
+
+    let result =
+      [
+        `/* istanbul ignore file */`,
+        `/* Auto generated file, do not edit by hand */`,
+      ].join('\n') + '\n\n';
+
+    const virtualPath = path.join(PROJECT_PATH, `__source.ts`);
+
+    let count = 0;
+    modules.forEach(moduleToGenerate => {
+      // Visit recursively collect types for each
+      // module and its dependencies.
+      const moduleAndChildrenGenerator = Visit(
+        moduleToGenerate,
+        virtualPath,
+        0,
+      );
+      for (let module of moduleAndChildrenGenerator) {
+        result += `declare module '${module.destinationSpecifier}' {\n`;
+        result += '\n\n';
+        result += module.body;
+        result += `\n}\n\n`;
+        count++;
+      }
+    });
+
+    if (count) {
+      logSync(`\n✅ Process finished for ${count} modules.`);
+    }
+
+    if (ERRORS.size) {
+      const messages = [...ERRORS.values()].map(err => `  ‣ ${err}`).join('\n');
+      logSync(`  Some errors occurred at:\n${messages}`);
+    }
+
+    return result;
+  }
+}
+
+function normalizePath(s: string) {
+  return s
+    .replace(/^@types\//, '')
+    .replace(/\/index(\.(jsx|ts|tsx|js))?$/, '')
+    .replace(/\.d\.ts$/, '')
+    .replace(/\/index$/, '')
+    .replace(/\/src\/?$/, '');
+}
diff --git a/scripts/core/utils.ts b/scripts/core/utils.ts
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..c2307ac4
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scripts/core/utils.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,337 @@
+import path from 'path';
+import isVersionGreater from 'semver/functions/gt';
+import ts from 'typescript';
+import { chalk, fs, glob } from 'zx';
+import { ScriptConfig } from '../config';
+
+/**
+ * create a terminal/CLI clickable link
+ * @param input
+ */
+export function createCLILink(input: {
+  line: number;
+  character: number;
+  filePath: string;
+  title: string;
+}) {
+  const { character, filePath, line, title } = input;
+
+  let targetUri = `file://${filePath}`;
+
+  if (line) {
+    targetUri += `:${line}`;
+    if (character) {
+      targetUri += `:${character}`;
+    }
+  }
+
+  const prefix = `\x1b]8;;${targetUri}\x07`;
+  const suffix = `\x1b]8;;\x07`;
+
+  return `${prefix}${title}${suffix}`;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Write text to the terminal.
+ * This is util because console.log is async
+ * @param value
+ * @param output
+ */
+export function logSync(
+  value: string,
+  output: 'stdout' | 'stderr' | 'warning' = 'stdout',
+) {
+  let formatted = value.endsWith('\n') ? value : value + '\n';
+  if (output === 'stderr') formatted = chalk.red(formatted);
+  if (output === 'warning') {
+    formatted = chalk.bgBlackBright(chalk.yellow(formatted));
+  }
+  const channel = output === 'warning' ? 'stdout' : output;
+  fs.writeSync(process[channel].fd, formatted);
+}
+
+export function escapeRegExp(string: string) {
+  return (
+    string
+      // https://github.com/sindresorhus/escape-string-regexp/blob/main/index.js
+      .replace(/[|\\{}()[\]^$+*?.]/g, '\\$&')
+      .replace(/-/g, '\\x2d')
+  );
+}
+
+// https://stackoverflow.com/a/7616484
+export function hashString(str: string) {
+  if (str.length === 0) return '0';
+  let hash = 0;
+  for (let i = 0; i < str.length; i++) {
+    const char = str.charCodeAt(i);
+    hash = (hash << 5) - hash + char;
+    hash |= 0;
+  }
+  return hash.toString(36).replace(/^-/, '');
+}
+
+// Throw an error if the valor is falsy
+export function invariant(
+  value: unknown,
+  error = 'Expected value to not be empty.',
+): asserts value {
+  if (!value) throw new Error(error);
+}
+
+export function lastElement<T>(parts: T[]): T {
+  invariant(parts.length, 'Expected array to not be empty');
+  return parts[parts.length - 1];
+}
+
+// Search on files to imports matching a specific string
+export namespace DiscoveryUsedImports {
+  export type Params = {
+    // the root folder to be scanned
+    folder: string;
+    // glob import declarations to be included
+    match: string[];
+    // glob imports to be ignored
+    ignore?: string[];
+  };
+
+  export function find(params: Params): string[] {
+    const { folder } = params;
+    const resolvedFolder = path.resolve(folder);
+    const filePaths = glob.sync(`${resolvedFolder}/**/*.{ts,tsx}`);
+    const elements = new Set<string>();
+
+    const testImport = createMatcher(params);
+
+    for (const filePath of filePaths) {
+      const fileContent = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');
+
+      const sourceFile = ts.createSourceFile(
+        filePath,
+        fileContent,
+        ts.ScriptTarget.Latest,
+        true,
+      );
+
+      function visit(node: ts.Node) {
+        const specifier = ts.isImportDeclaration(node)
+          ? ts.isStringLiteral(node.moduleSpecifier)
+            ? node.moduleSpecifier.text
+            : null
+          : null;
+        if (testImport(specifier)) elements.add(specifier);
+        ts.forEachChild(node, visit);
+      }
+
+      visit(sourceFile);
+    }
+
+    return [...elements];
+  }
+}
+
+// Creates a tester function from a glob pattern
+export function createMatcher(params: {
+  match: string | string[];
+  ignore?: string | string[];
+}) {
+  type NullableString = string | null | undefined;
+
+  function getPattern(glob: string) {
+    assertString(glob);
+
+    let regexString = '';
+    let i = 0;
+    let segment = '';
+
+    while (i < glob.length) {
+      const char = glob[i];
+
+      if (char === '*') {
+        if (segment) {
+          regexString += escapeRegExp(segment);
+          segment = '';
+        }
+
+        if (glob[i + 1] === '*') {
+          // Here, the current char is '*' and the next one is '*', then
+          // it is '**': match everything including slashes
+          regexString += '.*';
+          i += 2;
+        } else {
+          regexString += '[^/]*';
+          i++;
+        }
+      } else {
+        segment += char;
+        i++;
+      }
+    }
+
+    if (segment) {
+      regexString += escapeRegExp(segment);
+    }
+
+    return regexString;
+  }
+
+  function getTest(glob: NullableString | NullableString[]) {
+    let array = (Array.isArray(glob) ? glob : [glob]).filter(Boolean);
+    if (!array || !array.length) return () => false;
+    const pattern = array.map(glob => getPattern(glob!)).join('|');
+    const regex = new RegExp(`^${pattern}$`);
+    return function test(value: NullableString) {
+      if (typeof value !== 'string') return false;
+      return regex.test(value);
+    };
+  }
+
+  const testInclude = getTest(params.match);
+  const testExclude = getTest(params.ignore);
+
+  return function test(value: NullableString): value is string {
+    if (typeof value !== 'string') return false;
+    return testInclude(value) && !testExclude(value);
+  };
+}
+
+export function assertString(
+  value: unknown,
+  message?: string,
+): asserts value is string {
+  invariant(
+    'string' === typeof value,
+    message ?? `Expected value to be a string.; found ${typeof value} instead.`,
+  );
+}
+
+// Compare package versions between appSemPararv3 and our local package
+export namespace PackageVersionCheck {
+  export type PackageInfo = {
+    superAppVersion: string;
+    localVersion: string | null;
+    packageName: string;
+    isRemoteVersionNewer: boolean;
+    isLocalVersionNewer: boolean;
+    areDifferent: boolean;
+  };
+
+  export function init() {
+    const { SUPER_APP_PATH } = ScriptConfig;
+
+    const superAppPackageJSON = fs.readJSONSync(
+      path.resolve(SUPER_APP_PATH, 'package.json'),
+    ) as ScriptConfig.PackageJSON;
+
+    const localPackages = {
+      ...ScriptConfig.packageJSON.dependencies,
+      ...ScriptConfig.packageJSON.devDependencies,
+    };
+
+    const superAppPackages = {
+      ...superAppPackageJSON.dependencies,
+      ...superAppPackageJSON.devDependencies,
+    };
+
+    const notInSyncVersions = new Map<string, PackageInfo>();
+
+    const cleanVersion = (version: string) => version.replace(/^\D+/g, '');
+
+    function check(packageName: string): PackageInfo | null {
+      const superAppVersion = superAppPackages[packageName];
+      const localVersion = localPackages[packageName];
+
+      const bothHave = Boolean(superAppVersion && localVersion);
+
+      const areDifferent =
+        bothHave &&
+        superAppVersion.replace(/[\D.]/g, '') !==
+          localVersion.replace(/[\D.]/g, '');
+
+      const isLocalVersionNewer =
+        areDifferent &&
+        isVersionGreater(
+          cleanVersion(localVersion),
+          cleanVersion(superAppVersion),
+        );
+
+      const isRemoteVersionNewer =
+        areDifferent &&
+        isVersionGreater(
+          cleanVersion(superAppVersion),
+          cleanVersion(localVersion),
+        );
+
+      const checked: PackageInfo = {
+        localVersion,
+        packageName,
+        superAppVersion,
+        isRemoteVersionNewer,
+        isLocalVersionNewer,
+        areDifferent,
+      };
+
+      if (areDifferent) {
+        notInSyncVersions.set(packageName, checked);
+      }
+      return checked;
+    }
+
+    function getWarnings() {
+      const versions = [...notInSyncVersions.values()];
+
+      const outdated = versions.filter(el => el.isRemoteVersionNewer);
+      const newer = versions.filter(el => el.isLocalVersionNewer);
+
+      if (!outdated.length && !newer.length) return;
+
+      const greenVersion = (version: string) =>
+        chalk.bgGreen.greenBright(version);
+      const redVersion = (version: string) => chalk.bgRed.bgRedBright(version);
+
+      let text = chalk.yellow(
+        '‣ The following packages may be updated to match' +
+          ' the versions used by appSemPararv3:\n',
+      );
+
+      const tab = '   ';
+
+      if (newer.length) {
+        text += `\n🟡 These local versions are conflicting (newer) with the ones on appSemPararv3:\n`;
+        newer.forEach(value => {
+          text +=
+            tab +
+            `${value.packageName}@${redVersion(value.localVersion!)}${greenVersion(value.superAppVersion)}\n`;
+        });
+      }
+
+      if (outdated.length) {
+        text += `\n🟡 Sync outdated packages with appSemPararv3:\n`;
+        text += 'npm install \\\n';
+
+        const lastIndex = outdated.length - 1;
+
+        outdated.forEach((item, idx) => {
+          const suffix = idx === lastIndex ? '' : ' \\';
+          text +=
+            tab +
+            `'${item.packageName}@${greenVersion(item.superAppVersion)}'${suffix}\n`;
+        });
+      }
+
+      if (newer.length && outdated.length) {
+        text +=
+          '\n' +
+          chalk.bgYellow.yellowBright(` △ There are 2 warnings above △ `);
+      }
+
+      return text;
+    }
+
+    return {
+      check,
+      getWarnings,
+      outdatedPackages: notInSyncVersions,
+    };
+  }
+}

diff --git a/scripts/plugins/prettier-plugin-line-break.mjs b/scripts/plugins/prettier-plugin-line-break.mjs
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..931e152d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scripts/plugins/prettier-plugin-line-break.mjs
@@ -0,0 +1,326 @@
+/**
+ * Organiza line breakers para padronizar código e facilitar code review.
+ */
+
+import { doc } from 'prettier';
+import * as estreePlugin from 'prettier/plugins/estree';
+
+const estreePrinters = estreePlugin.printers;
+
+const originalEstreePrinter = estreePrinters.estree;
+const { group, indent, line, softline, join, ifBreak } = doc.builders;
+
+const DEFAULT_THRESHOLD = 1;
+const DEFAULT_CONDITION_PRINT_WIDTH = 50;
+
+const FUNCTION_NODE_TYPES = new Set([
+  'FunctionDeclaration',
+  'FunctionExpression',
+  'ArrowFunctionExpression',
+  'ObjectMethod',
+  'ClassMethod',
+  'TSDeclareMethod',
+  'TSFunctionType',
+  'TSMethodSignature',
+]);
+
+const TYPE_ASSERTION_TYPES = new Set([
+  'TSAsExpression',
+  'TSTypeAssertion',
+]);
+
+function isArrowBodyObject(path) {
+  const parent = path.getParentNode();
+  return (
+    parent?.type === 'ArrowFunctionExpression' &&
+    parent.body === path.getValue()
+  );
+}
+
+function isArrowBodyTypeAssertion(path) {
+  const node = path.getValue();
+  const parent = path.getParentNode();
+
+  if (parent?.type !== 'ArrowFunctionExpression' || parent.body !== node) {
+    return false;
+  }
+
+  let current = node;
+  while (TYPE_ASSERTION_TYPES.has(current?.type)) {
+    current = current.expression;
+  }
+
+  return current?.type === 'ObjectExpression';
+}
+
+function asInt(value, defaultValue) {
+  const num = parseInt(String(value), 10);
+  if (!Number.isNaN(num) && num >= 0) {
+    return num;
+  }
+  return defaultValue;
+}
+
+function createWrappedPrinter(originalPrinter) {
+  return {
+    ...originalPrinter,
+    print(path, options, print) {
+      const node = path.getValue();
+
+      const getText = () => {
+        if (!node.range || !options.originalText) return '';
+        return options.originalText.slice(node.range[0], node.range[1]);
+      };
+
+      const multilineWrapThreshold = asInt(
+        options.multilineWrapThreshold,
+        DEFAULT_THRESHOLD,
+      );
+      const objectWrapThreshold = asInt(
+        options.objectWrapThreshold,
+        multilineWrapThreshold,
+      );
+      const arrayWrapThreshold = asInt(
+        options.arrayWrapThreshold,
+        objectWrapThreshold,
+      );
+      const paramsPrintWidth = asInt(
+        options.paramsPrintWidth,
+        DEFAULT_CONDITION_PRINT_WIDTH,
+      );
+
+      const trailingComma =
+        options.trailingComma === 'all' || options.trailingComma === 'es5'
+          ? ifBreak(',', '')
+          : '';
+
+      if (
+        node?.type === 'IfStatement' &&
+        node.consequent &&
+        node.consequent.type !== 'BlockStatement' &&
+        getText().split(')')[0].length > paramsPrintWidth
+      ) {
+        const parts = [
+          'if (',
+          softline,
+          path.call(print, 'test'),
+          softline,
+          ') ',
+          '{',
+          indent([
+            line,
+            path.call(print, 'consequent'),
+          ]),
+          line,
+          '}',
+        ];
+
+        if (node.alternate) {
+          parts.push(' else ', path.call(print, 'alternate'));
+        }
+
+        return parts;
+      }
+
+      if (
+        TYPE_ASSERTION_TYPES.has(node?.type) &&
+        isArrowBodyTypeAssertion(path)
+      ) {
+        return [
+          '(',
+          originalPrinter.print(path, options, print),
+          ')',
+        ];
+      }
+
+      if (
+        node?.type === 'ArrayExpression' &&
+        node.elements &&
+        node.elements.length > arrayWrapThreshold
+      ) {
+        return group(
+          [
+            '[',
+            indent([
+              line,
+              join(
+                [
+                  ',',
+                  line,
+                ],
+                path.map(print, 'elements'),
+              ),
+              trailingComma,
+            ]),
+            line,
+            ']',
+          ],
+          { shouldBreak: true },
+        );
+      }
+
+      if (
+        node?.type === 'ObjectExpression' &&
+        node.properties &&
+        node.properties.length > objectWrapThreshold
+      ) {
+        const objectDoc = group(
+          [
+            '{',
+            indent([
+              line,
+              join(
+                [
+                  ',',
+                  line,
+                ],
+                path.map(print, 'properties'),
+              ),
+              trailingComma,
+            ]),
+            line,
+            '}',
+          ],
+          { shouldBreak: true },
+        );
+
+        if (isArrowBodyObject(path)) {
+          return [
+            '(',
+            objectDoc,
+            ')',
+          ];
+        }
+
+        return objectDoc;
+      }
+
+      if (
+        node?.type === 'TSTypeLiteral' &&
+        node.members &&
+        node.members.length > objectWrapThreshold
+      ) {
+        return group(
+          [
+            '{',
+            indent([
+              line,
+              join(line, path.map(print, 'members')),
+            ]),
+            line,
+            '}',
+          ],
+          {
+            shouldBreak: true,
+          },
+        );
+      }
+
+      const shouldBreakParams =
+        FUNCTION_NODE_TYPES.has(node?.type) &&
+        node.params &&
+        node.params.length > 3;
+
+      if (shouldBreakParams) {
+        const result = [];
+
+        if (
+          node.type === 'ClassMethod' ||
+          node.type === 'ObjectMethod' ||
+          node.type === 'TSDeclareMethod'
+        ) {
+          if (node.static) result.push('static ');
+          if (node.accessibility) result.push(node.accessibility, ' ');
+          if (node.abstract) result.push('abstract ');
+          if (node.kind === 'get' || node.kind === 'set') {
+            result.push(node.kind, ' ');
+          }
+        }
+
+        if (node.async) {
+          result.push('async ');
+        }
+
+        if (
+          node.type === 'FunctionDeclaration' ||
+          node.type === 'FunctionExpression'
+        ) {
+          if (getText().match(/^(async )?function/)) {
+            result.push('function');
+          }
+
+          if (node.generator) {
+            result.push('*');
+          }
+          if (node.id) {
+            result.push(' ', path.call(print, 'id'));
+          }
+        } else if (node.key) {
+          result.push(path.call(print, 'key'));
+        }
+
+        if (node.typeParameters) {
+          result.push(path.call(print, 'typeParameters'));
+        }
+
+        result.push(
+          group(
+            [
+              '(',
+              indent([
+                line,
+                join(
+                  [
+                    ',',
+                    line,
+                  ],
+                  path.map(print, 'params'),
+                ),
+              ]),
+              line,
+              ')',
+            ],
+            {
+              shouldBreak: true,
+            },
+          ),
+        );
+
+        if (node.returnType) {
+          result.push(path.call(print, 'returnType'));
+        } else if (node.predicate) {
+          result.push(path.call(print, 'predicate'));
+        }
+
+        if (node.body) {
+          const shouldAddBraces = node.body.type !== 'BlockStatement';
+
+          if (node.type === 'ArrowFunctionExpression') {
+            if (shouldAddBraces) {
+              result.push('=> {', line, 'return ');
+            } else {
+              result.push('=>');
+            }
+          }
+
+          result.push(' ', path.call(print, 'body'));
+          if (shouldAddBraces) {
+            result.push(line, '}', line);
+          }
+        }
+
+        return result;
+      }
+
+      return originalPrinter.print(path, options, print);
+    },
+  };
+}
+
+const plugin = {
+  printers: {
+    estree: createWrappedPrinter(originalEstreePrinter),
+  },
+};
+
+export const printers = plugin.printers;
diff --git a/scripts/sync-typings.ts b/scripts/sync-typings.ts
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..496cf0c9
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scripts/sync-typings.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@
+import path from 'path';
+import { fs } from 'zx';
+import { Generator } from './core/generate-types';
+
+import * as process from 'node:process';
+import { ScriptConfig } from './config';
+import { Formatter } from './core/formatter';
+import {
+  DiscoveryUsedImports,
+  PackageVersionCheck,
+  invariant,
+  logSync,
+} from './core/utils';
+
+/**
+ * This script is used to generate types from appSemPararv3 and
+ * check package version mismatching between appSemPararv3 and our
+ * local package
+ */
+async function main() {
+  // prettier-ignore
+  const {
+    SUPER_APP_PATH,
+    MICRO_FRONTEND_SRC,
+    CWD,
+    syncSuperAppTypesConfig
+  } = ScriptConfig;
+
+  invariant(SUPER_APP_PATH);
+
+  if (!fs.existsSync(SUPER_APP_PATH)) {
+    return logSync(
+      `🔴 Sync types skipped. Failed to find the Super App code at ${SUPER_APP_PATH}`,
+      'stderr',
+    );
+  }
+
+  // Searching for all imports from appSemPararv3 that are
+  // used in our package (the ones  starting with `~/`)
+  const usedImportsFromRoot = DiscoveryUsedImports.find(
+    syncSuperAppTypesConfig,
+  );
+
+  if (!usedImportsFromRoot.length) {
+    throw new Error(
+      `Failed to find imports matching '~/*' at ${MICRO_FRONTEND_SRC}/*`,
+    );
+  }
+
+  usedImportsFromRoot.push('~/routes/Models');
+
+  const versionCheck = PackageVersionCheck.init();
+
+  const warning = `Generating types from ${SUPER_APP_PATH}`;
+  logSync(
+    `${'='.repeat(warning.length)}\n${warning}\n⚠️Make sure the project is up to date.\n${'='.repeat(warning.length)}`,
+  );
+
+  let declarations = Generator.Generate({
+    modules: usedImportsFromRoot,
+    projectPath: SUPER_APP_PATH,
+    transform(el) {
+      if (el.packageName === ScriptConfig.packageJSON.name) {
+        // prevent overwriting typings for our own package
+        return null;
+      }
+
+      // handling types from node_modules
+      if (el.isNodeModule) {
+        versionCheck.check(el.packageName);
+        return null;
+      }
+
+      return el;
+    },
+  });
+
+  try {
+    declarations = await Formatter.Format(declarations);
+  } catch (e: any) {
+    logSync(e.message, 'stderr');
+  }
+
+  const outputPath = path.resolve(CWD, '@types/super-app.d.ts');
+  fs.rmSync(outputPath, {
+    recursive: true,
+    force: true,
+  });
+  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(outputPath), { recursive: true });
+  fs.writeFileSync(outputPath, declarations);
+
+  console.log(
+    `\n‣ Type definitions saved at ./${path.relative(CWD, outputPath)}`,
+  );
+
+  const versionWarnings = versionCheck.getWarnings();
+  if (versionWarnings) {
+    console.log(versionWarnings);
+  }
+}
+
+main().catch(reason => {
+  logSync(reason.message || reason?.toString?.() || 'Unknown error', 'stderr');
+  const onClose = () => process.exit(1);
+  process.stdout.once('drain', onClose);
+  process.stderr.once('drain', onClose);
+  setTimeout(onClose, 2000);
+});
diff --git a/scripts/typescript-check.ts b/scripts/typescript-check.ts
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..fe8171a2
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scripts/typescript-check.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,159 @@
+/* istanbul ignore file */
+import path from 'path';
+import ts from 'typescript';
+import { createCLILink, logSync } from './core/utils';
+
+/**
+ * This script provides a workaround for a specific TypeScript limitation.
+ * By default, `tsc` does not offer an option to ignore type errors originating
+ * from imported source files (`.ts`/`.tsx`) located in `node_modules`.
+ * Our project imports source code directly from `@semparar/*` packages,
+ * which makes this script necessary to achieve a clean type check.
+ * @see https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/47387
+ */
+
+const CWD = process.cwd();
+const INCLUDE = [path.join(CWD, 'src')];
+const ONLY_ERRORS = true;
+
+async function main() {
+  logSync(`Typing check started...\n`);
+
+  const configPath = ts.findConfigFile(CWD, ts.sys.fileExists, 'tsconfig.json');
+
+  if (!configPath) throw new Error('tsconfig.json not found');
+
+  const configFile = ts.readConfigFile(configPath, ts.sys.readFile);
+
+  const { options, fileNames } = ts.parseJsonConfigFileContent(
+    configFile.config,
+    ts.sys,
+    path.dirname(configPath),
+    { noEmit: true },
+  );
+
+  const program = ts.createProgram({
+    rootNames: fileNames,
+    options,
+  });
+
+  type ParsedDiagnostic = ts.Diagnostic & {
+    message: string;
+    position: {
+      link: string;
+      line: number;
+      character: number;
+      filePath: string;
+      relativePath: string;
+    };
+  };
+
+  const diagnosticByFile: Record<string, ParsedDiagnostic[]> = {};
+
+  const allDiagnostics = ts.getPreEmitDiagnostics(program);
+
+  const parsedDiagnostics: ParsedDiagnostic[] = [];
+
+  allDiagnostics.forEach(diagnostic => {
+    if (ONLY_ERRORS && diagnostic.category !== ts.DiagnosticCategory.Error) {
+      return;
+    }
+
+    const location = (() => {
+      const { file } = diagnostic;
+
+      if (!file) {
+        return {
+          line: 0,
+          character: 0,
+          filePath: CWD,
+          relativePath: CWD,
+          isGenericError: true,
+        };
+      }
+
+      const { line: l, character: c } = file.getLineAndCharacterOfPosition(
+        diagnostic.start!,
+      );
+
+      return {
+        line: l + 1,
+        character: c + 1,
+        filePath: file.fileName,
+        relativePath: path.relative(CWD, file.fileName),
+        isGenericError: false,
+      };
+    })();
+
+    const shouldIncludeReport =
+      location.isGenericError ||
+      INCLUDE.some(include => location.filePath.startsWith(include));
+
+    if (!shouldIncludeReport) return;
+
+    const { relativePath, line, character, isGenericError } = location;
+
+    const link = isGenericError
+      ? CWD
+      : `./${relativePath} (${line},${character})`;
+
+    const message = ts.flattenDiagnosticMessageText(
+      diagnostic.messageText,
+      '\n',
+    );
+
+    const parsedDiagnostic: ParsedDiagnostic = {
+      ...diagnostic,
+      message,
+      position: {
+        ...location,
+        link,
+      },
+    };
+    diagnosticByFile[relativePath] = diagnosticByFile[relativePath] || [];
+    diagnosticByFile[relativePath].push(parsedDiagnostic);
+    parsedDiagnostics.push(parsedDiagnostic);
+  });
+
+  if (!parsedDiagnostics.length) return;
+
+  const sortedByErrorCount = Object.values(diagnosticByFile).sort(
+    (a, b) => a.length - b.length,
+  );
+
+  sortedByErrorCount.forEach(items => {
+    logSync(`\n${items[0].position.link}:\n`, 'stderr');
+
+    items.forEach(({ message, position: { line, character, filePath } }) => {
+      message = message.split('\n')[0].trim();
+
+      message = ` ‣ (${line}:${character}): ${message}`;
+
+      if (message.length > 80) {
+        message = `${message.slice(0, 79)}…`;
+      }
+
+      const url = createCLILink({
+        title: `${message}\n`,
+        line,
+        filePath,
+        character,
+      });
+
+      logSync(url, 'stderr');
+    });
+  });
+
+  throw new Error(
+    `Typescript found ${parsedDiagnostics.length} errors in ${sortedByErrorCount.length} files.`,
+  );
+}
+
+main().catch(error => {
+  if (error?.message) {
+    logSync(`\n${error.message}\n\n`, 'stderr');
+  }
+  setTimeout(() => {
+    process.exit(1);
+  }, 1000);
+});

+  arrow1Fade: Animated.Value;

diff --git a/src/atomic/atoms/AnimationArrows/index.tsx b/src/atomic/atoms/AnimationArrows/index.tsx
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..e1873fd2
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/atomic/atoms/AnimationArrows/index.tsx
@@ -0,0 +1,117 @@
+import { useIsFocused } from '@react-navigation/native';
+import React, { useEffect, useRef } from 'react';
+import { Animated } from 'react-native';
+
+import AnimationArrowsView from './view';
+
+const AnimationArrows = () => {
+  const isFocused = useIsFocused();
+  const arrow1Move = useRef(new Animated.Value(85)).current;
+  const arrow1Fade = useRef(new Animated.Value(0.001)).current;
+  const arrow2Move = useRef(new Animated.Value(85)).current;
+  const arrow2Fade = useRef(new Animated.Value(0.001)).current;
+  const arrow3Fade = useRef(new Animated.Value(0.001)).current;
+  const arrow3Move = useRef(new Animated.Value(85)).current;
+
+  const ArrowAnimate = () => {
+    Animated.loop(
+      Animated.sequence([
+        Animated.parallel([
+          Animated.parallel([
+            Animated.timing(arrow2Move, {
+              toValue: -80,
+              duration: 2500,
+              useNativeDriver: true,
+            }),
+            Animated.sequence([
+              Animated.timing(arrow2Fade, {
+                toValue: 1,
+                duration: 1250,
+                useNativeDriver: true,
+              }),
+              Animated.timing(arrow2Fade, {
+                toValue: 0,
+                duration: 1250,
+                useNativeDriver: true,
+              }),
+            ]),
+          ]),
+          Animated.parallel([
+            Animated.timing(arrow1Move, {
+              delay: 450,
+              toValue: -50,
+              duration: 2050,
+              useNativeDriver: true,
+            }),
+            Animated.sequence([
+              Animated.timing(arrow1Fade, {
+                delay: 450,
+                toValue: 1,
+                duration: 800,
+                useNativeDriver: true,
+              }),
+              Animated.timing(arrow1Fade, {
+                toValue: 0,
+                duration: 800,
+                useNativeDriver: true,
+              }),
+            ]),
+          ]),
+          Animated.parallel([
+            Animated.timing(arrow3Move, {
+              delay: 750,
+              toValue: -20,
+              duration: 1750,
+              useNativeDriver: true,
+            }),
+            Animated.sequence([
+              Animated.timing(arrow3Fade, {
+                delay: 600,
+                toValue: 1,
+                duration: 800,
+                useNativeDriver: true,
+              }),
+              Animated.timing(arrow3Fade, {
+                toValue: 0,
+                duration: 800,
+                useNativeDriver: true,
+              }),
+            ]),
+          ]),
+        ]),
+      ]),
+    ).start();
+  };
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    if (!isFocused) {
+      arrow1Move.stopAnimation();
+      arrow2Move.stopAnimation();
+      arrow3Move.stopAnimation();
+      arrow1Fade.stopAnimation();
+      arrow2Fade.stopAnimation();
+      arrow3Fade.stopAnimation();
+    } else {
+      arrow1Move.setValue(85);
+      arrow2Move.setValue(85);
+      arrow3Move.setValue(85);
+      arrow1Fade.setValue(0.001);
+      arrow2Fade.setValue(0.001);
+      arrow3Fade.setValue(0.001);
+      ArrowAnimate();
+    }
+  }, [isFocused]);
+
+  return (
+    <AnimationArrowsView
+      arrow1Move={arrow1Move}
+      arrow1Fade={arrow1Fade}
+      arrow2Move={arrow2Move}
+      arrow2Fade={arrow2Fade}
+      arrow3Move={arrow3Move}
+      arrow3Fade={arrow3Fade}
+    />
+  );
+};
+
+export default AnimationArrows;

index 00000000..803cddee
--- /dev/null

@@ -0,0 +1,90 @@
+import { Box, Image } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
+import { Text } from '@semparar/design-system-app/fintech';
+import React from 'react';
+import { Animated } from 'react-native';
+import arrow from '../../../assets/images/arrow.png';
+import { AnimationArrowsViewProps } from './Models';
+import { Container } from './styles';
+
+const AnimationArrowsView = ({
+  arrow1Move,
+  arrow1Fade,
+  arrow2Move,
+  arrow2Fade,
+  arrow3Move,
+  arrow3Fade,
+}: AnimationArrowsViewProps) => {
+  return (
+    <Container testID="AnimationArrowsView">
+      <Box
+        justify="center"
+        align="center"
+        width="100%"
+        overflow="hidden"
+        height={175}
+      >
+        <Box direction justify="center">
+          <Animated.View
+            style={[
+              {
+                position: 'absolute',
+                transform: [
+                  { translateX: -35 },
+                  { translateY: arrow1Move },
+                ],
+                opacity: arrow1Fade,
+              },
+            ]}
+          >
+            <Image
+              height={41}
+              width={30}
+              /* @ts-ignore `source` is not in the props Of `image` ( ͡° ͜ʖ ͡°) */
+              source={arrow}
+            />
+          </Animated.View>
+          <Animated.View
+            style={[
+              {
+                position: 'absolute',
+                transform: [{ translateY: arrow2Move }],
+                opacity: arrow2Fade,
+              },
+            ]}
+          >
+            <Image
+              height={41}
+              width={30}
+              /* @ts-ignore `source` is not in the props Of `image` ( ͡° ͜ʖ ͡°) */
+              source={arrow}
+            />
+          </Animated.View>
+          <Animated.View
+            style={[
+              {
+                position: 'absolute',
+                transform: [
+                  { translateX: 30 },
+                  { translateY: arrow3Move },
+                ],
+                opacity: arrow3Fade,
+              },
+            ]}
+          >
+            <Image
+              height={41}
+              width={30}
+              /* @ts-ignore `source` is not in the props Of `image` ( ͡° ͜ʖ ͡°) */
+              source={arrow}
+            />
+          </Animated.View>
+        </Box>
+      </Box>
+      <Text align="center" mt="middle" type="subtitleLarger">
+        Estamos carregando suas informações...
+      </Text>
+    </Container>
+  );
+};
+
+export default AnimationArrowsView;

--- /dev/null

@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+import { NavigationProp, useNavigation } from '@react-navigation/native';
+import React, { FC } from 'react';
+
+import { PropsRootStack } from '../../../routes/Models';
+import { ApiErrorProps } from './Models';
+import ApiErrorView from './view';
+
+const ApiError: FC<ApiErrorProps> = ({
+  onPress,
+  subTitle = 'Por favor, espere alguns minutos ou tente novamente.',
+  title = 'Ocorreu um erro inesperado',
+  hasBackToHome = true,
+  isLoading = false,
+  testID = 'ApiErrorView',
+  targetScreen = 'Home',
+}) => {
+  const navigation = useNavigation<NavigationProp<PropsRootStack>>();
+
+  const handleNavigateToHome = () => {
+    navigation.navigate(targetScreen as never);
+  };
+
+  return (
+    <ApiErrorView
+      testID={testID}
+      subTitle={subTitle}
+      title={title}
+      onPress={onPress}
+      hasBackToHome={hasBackToHome}
+      isLoading={isLoading}
+      handleNavigateToHome={handleNavigateToHome}
+    />
+  );
+};
+
+export default ApiError;

@@ -0,0 +1,85 @@
+import { Box, Button } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
+import { Text } from '@semparar/design-system-app/fintech';
+import React, { FC } from 'react';
+import { Image } from 'react-native';
+
+import { Icon } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
+import ErrorApi from '../../../assets/images/errorApi.png';
+import { ApiErrorViewProps } from './Models';
+import { Container, Content } from './styles';
+
+const ApiErrorView: FC<ApiErrorViewProps> = ({
+  onPress,
+  title,
+  subTitle,
+  hasBackToHome,
+  isLoading,
+  handleNavigateToHome,
+  testID,
+}) => {
+  return (
+    <Container testID={testID}>
+      <Content>
+        {hasBackToHome ? (
+          <Box width={100} align="center">
+            <Image
+              source={ErrorApi}
+              style={{
+                width: 188,
+                height: 188,
+              }}
+            />
+          </Box>
+        ) : (
+          <Box
+            bgColor="DENY_COLOR_03"
+            borderColor="DENY_COLOR_01"
+            borderRadiusBottomLeft="medium"
+            pd="innerMedium"
+            borderRadiusBottomRight="medium"
+            borderRadiusTopRight="medium"
+            borderRadiusTopLeft="medium"
+            borderBottomWidth="small"
+            borderTopWidth="small"
+            borderLeftWidth="small"
+            borderRightWidth="small"
+          >
+            <Icon name="icon-circle-alert" color="DENY_COLOR_01" size="sm" />
+          </Box>
+        )}
+
+        <Text mt="smaller" type="subtitleXXXXL" align="center">
+          {title}
+        </Text>
+
+        {!!subTitle && (
+          <Text
+            align="center"
+            mt="xxxxs"
+            type="paragraphXXL"
+            color="INTENSE_COLOR_03"
+          >
+            {subTitle}
+          </Text>
+        )}
+      </Content>
+      <Button
+        testID="apiError_retry-button"
+        isLoading={isLoading}
+        text={hasBackToHome ? 'Tentar novamente' : 'Voltar'}
+        mb="xs"
+        onPress={onPress}
+      />
+      {hasBackToHome && (
+        <Button
+          mb="xs"
+          type="ghostSmall"
+          onPress={handleNavigateToHome}
+          text="Voltar para a home"
+        />
+      )}
+    </Container>
+  );
+};
+
+export default ApiErrorView;
